name: Home
title: Home – Art Education Bookings
description: Homepage for discovering and accessing art classes, workshops, online courses, passes, and gift cards, with global search, quick actions, and featured content.

core_features: Check authentication status on load to conditionally show account-related navigation behavior (My Bookings, Wishlist) using getAuthStatus.
Allow the user to log in via a credential form, updating access to account features, using loginWithCredentials.

Provide a global search bar that searches across classes, workshops, courses, and passes by keyword using searchGlobalOfferings, with inline preview results and navigation to the full classes listing.

Display homepage quick actions (e.g., book a class, online classes, buy a pass, purchase a gift card) using getHomeQuickActions and map them to appropriate navigation targets including classes.html, passes.html, and gift_cards.html.
Show featured content sections (featured classes, online courses, kids’ classes, workshops, passes, and special offers) using getHomeFeaturedContent to help the user discover relevant offerings.

Offer quick navigation links in the header to core areas: Classes, Online Classes, Kids & Teens, Workshops, Passes & Packages, Gift Cards, My Bookings, Wishlist, and Cart, with some links gated by authentication where appropriate.
user_workflows:
Workflow: Check login state and access account pages
1. When the Home page loads, the auth-status-checker component calls getAuthStatus.
2. If getAuthStatus.isAuthenticated is true, the header nav enables direct navigation for My Bookings and Wishlist links (e.g., my_bookings.html, wishlist.html) without interception.
3. If getAuthStatus.isAuthenticated is false, clicking My Bookings or Wishlist triggers the login-inline-form to open (by updating the URL to include a showLogin flag) instead of navigating immediately.
4. The user submits the login form, which calls loginWithCredentials with the provided username and password.
5. On successful login (loginWithCredentials.isAuthenticated true), the component updates the URL to remove the showLogin flag, optionally re-calls getAuthStatus, and then proceeds with navigation to the originally requested account page.
6. On failed login, the component shows the returned message from loginWithCredentials.message and allows the user to retry.

Workflow: Perform a global search and view inline results
1. The user types a keyword in the global-search-form input.
2. On submit, the component updates the URL query string to include a search parameter (e.g., ?q={encodedQuery}) without leaving the page.
3. The global-search-form calls searchGlobalOfferings with query = current search term and limit = a configurable preview result limit.
4. When results return, the search-results-preview-list displays classResults (mapped to ClassOffering) and passResults (mapped to PassProduct) in separate sections.
5. The user can click an individual class result to navigate to a class detail page (e.g., class_detail.html?offeringId={offeringId}) or a pass result to navigate to a pass detail page (e.g., pass_detail.html?passProductId={passProductId}).
6. Alternatively, the user can click a 'View all results' control, which navigates to classes.html with categoryId=all_classes and query={encodedQuery} as URL parameters.

Workflow: Use quick actions to reach key booking tasks
1. On page load, the quick-actions-panel calls getHomeQuickActions.
2. For each returned quick action, the panel renders a button or link labeled by the action’s label property.
3. When the user clicks a quick action:
   - If actionCode corresponds to booking general classes (e.g., book_a_class), the component navigates to classes.html?categoryId=all_classes.
   - If actionCode corresponds to online classes (e.g., online_classes), the component navigates to classes.html?categoryId=online_classes.
   - If actionCode corresponds to buying a pass (e.g., buy_a_pass), the component navigates to passes.html.
   - If actionCode corresponds to purchasing a gift card (e.g., purchase_a_gift_card), the component navigates to gift_cards.html.
4. The resulting page uses its own logic to complete the chosen task.

Workflow: Discover offerings via featured content
1. On page load, the featured-content-loader (implicit container) calls getHomeFeaturedContent once.
2. The response is distributed to the featured-classes-section, featured-online-courses-section, featured-kids-classes-section, featured-workshops-section, featured-passes-section, and special-offers-list.
3. Each section lists the corresponding items (e.g., featuredClasses and featuredPasses) using their provided properties such as title, subtitle, categoryName, basePrice, isFree, ratingAverage, labelBadges, and time/location summaries.
4. When a user clicks a featured class/workshop/online course/kids’ class item, the component navigates to the relevant class detail page with offeringId in the URL (e.g., class_detail.html?offeringId={offeringId}).
5. When a user clicks a featured pass item, the component navigates to a pass detail or listing page using the passProductId in the URL (e.g., pass_detail.html?passProductId={passProductId}).
6. For each special offer, the user can see the title, description, and promoCode; interactions may include copying the promoCode to the clipboard or exposing it in the UI so the user can manually apply it during checkout on other pages.

Workflow: Resume an existing search via URL parameter
1. A user lands on Home with a URL that already includes a search query parameter (e.g., /index.html?q={encodedQuery}).
2. On initialization, the global-search-form reads the q parameter and pre-populates the search input with its decoded value.
3. The component immediately calls searchGlobalOfferings with that query and the configured preview limit.
4. The search-results-preview-list displays the returned results without further input from the user.
5. The user may refine the search term and submit again, which updates the q parameter and re-triggers searchGlobalOfferings.
interactions: Clicking a main navigation link (Classes, Online Classes, Kids & Teens, Workshops, Passes & Packages, Gift Cards, My Bookings, Wishlist, Cart) updates window.location to the corresponding page URL, optionally appending URL parameters like categoryId or query as specified.

Submitting the login-inline-form triggers loginWithCredentials with the form’s username and password values; a successful response updates authentication-dependent UI state and can trigger redirect; a failed response displays the message field.

Clicking My Bookings or Wishlist while unauthenticated triggers an update of the URL to include a login flag (e.g., ?showLogin=true), which causes the login-inline-form to be displayed instead of navigating immediately.

Typing in the global search field and pressing enter (or clicking a search button) triggers a call to searchGlobalOfferings with the current input value and a configured preview limit, and synchronizes the q URL parameter with the same value.
Clicking 'View all results' beneath the search preview navigates the user to classes.html with URL parameters categoryId=all_classes and query set to the current search term.

Clicking a search preview class result navigates to a class detail URL that includes the offeringId as a URL parameter; clicking a pass result navigates to a pass detail URL with passProductId as a URL parameter.

Clicking a quick action button triggers navigation mapped from its actionCode: general booking flows go to classes.html with an appropriate categoryId parameter; passes go to passes.html; gift cards go to gift_cards.html.

Clicking a featured class, workshop, online course, or kids’ class card navigates to its detail page with offeringId as a URL parameter; clicking a featured pass navigates to its detail page with passProductId as a URL parameter.

Interacting with special offers (e.g., clicking a 'copy code' control within the special-offers-list) does not call any additional interfaces but exposes the promoCode string so the user can use it on relevant checkout pages.
page_functionality.state_logic:
The Home page does not declare incoming navigation parameters for routing between pages, but it uses URL query parameters to manage its own internal state:
- Search state: A query parameter (e.g., q) holds the current global search term. On initial load, if q is present, the global-search-form initializes the search input with its value and immediately calls searchGlobalOfferings(query=q, limit=PREVIEW_LIMIT). When the user submits a new search, the component updates q in the URL (pushing or replacing history) and then calls searchGlobalOfferings with the updated value.
- Login visibility state: A query parameter (e.g., showLogin) controls whether the login-inline-form is shown. When an unauthenticated user clicks My Bookings or Wishlist, the header-primary-nav appends or updates showLogin in the URL (such as showLogin=true) instead of navigating away. On load or URL change, the login component checks this flag to decide if the login interface should be visible. After a successful login, the flag is removed from the URL, and navigation may proceed to the originally intended destination.
- No persistent state is stored in localStorage; all transient, shareable state that needs to survive reloads or deep-linking is reflected in the URL query string. The page uses only the assigned interfaces (getAuthStatus, loginWithCredentials, getHomeFeaturedContent, searchGlobalOfferings, getHomeQuickActions) and does not cache or synthesize data outside of what is returned by these APIs.

components.functionality: Renders the main site navigation, including links to Home, Classes, Online Classes, Kids & Teens, Workshops, Passes & Packages, Gift Cards, My Bookings, Wishlist, and Cart. It coordinates with auth state to decide whether to navigate directly to account pages or to prompt for login.

On page load, checks whether the user is authenticated and exposes this state to other components (such as header-primary-nav and login-inline-form) so they can adjust behavior for My Bookings and Wishlist links.

Provides a credential-based login form that is shown when the URL indicates a login is needed (e.g., showLogin parameter is set) or when the user attempts to access restricted navigation items while unauthenticated. It authenticates the user using loginWithCredentials and, on success, updates authentication state and can redirect to the originally requested destination.

Displays the global search input for classes, workshops, courses, and passes. It syncs the search term with the URL, calls searchGlobalOfferings for inline results, and exposes actions to view all results in the classes listing.
Shows a limited set of search results from searchGlobalOfferings, separated into classResults and passResults, and enables navigation to detailed pages for each result.

Loads quick actions using getHomeQuickActions and renders them as prominent buttons that navigate the user directly to key flows like booking a class, viewing online classes, buying a pass, or purchasing a gift card.

Central component that calls getHomeFeaturedContent once on page load and distributes the response data to the various featured sections (classes, online courses, kids’ classes, workshops, passes, and special offers).

Displays the list of featuredClasses from getHomeFeaturedContent, including title, subtitle, categoryName, pricing, rating, badges, and schedule/location summaries. Allows navigation to each class’s detail page.

Displays featuredOnlineCourses from getHomeFeaturedContent in a similar way to the general featured classes, emphasizing that they are online and enabling navigation to the corresponding class detail pages.
Displays featuredKidsClasses from getHomeFeaturedContent, focusing on offerings with kids/teens as the audience. Enables navigation to each offering’s detail page.
Displays featuredWorkshops from getHomeFeaturedContent, highlighting workshop-format offerings. Enables navigation to each workshop’s detail page.

Displays featuredPasses from getHomeFeaturedContent, summarizing each pass product’s name, categoryName, numClassesIncluded, price, and timeOfDayRestriction, and allowing users to navigate to corresponding pass details.
Renders specialOffers from getHomeFeaturedContent, showing titles, descriptions, and promo codes. Supports simple interactions such as revealing or copying promo codes for use on other pages.

name: Class Listings
title: Class Listings
description: Search, filter, sort, and browse all available classes, workshops, online courses, and kids’ classes, with wishlist and workshop comparison tools.

core_features: Determine listing context from the URL categoryId parameter and initialize filters for that context using getClassListingFilterOptions.
Load the user’s authentication state with getAuthStatus to enable or disable wishlist actions.
Execute class searches with searchClassOfferings using the current listingCategory, query, filter parameters, sortBy, and page.
Provide a keyword search input bound to the query URL parameter that refines results via searchClassOfferings.

Expose filter controls (level, audience, age range, date range, day-of-week, time-of-day, location, price, rating, class type, label badges) bound to URL parameters and used to build the filters argument for searchClassOfferings.
Provide sort controls bound to the sortBy URL parameter that are passed to searchClassOfferings.
Render a paginated list of class offerings using the results from searchClassOfferings, with navigation via the page URL parameter.
Allow logged-in users to add a class offering to the wishlist from the listing view using addClassOfferingToWishlist, updating relevant counts or feedback.
Enable selecting workshops for comparison via addWorkshopToCompare and removeWorkshopFromCompare directly from class cards when canAddToCompare is true.
Display and maintain a compare bar using getWorkshopCompareState and clearWorkshopCompareList, and navigate to compare.html when the user chooses to compare.
Navigate to class_detail.html with the selected offeringId when a user clicks a class card.
user_workflows:
Initial page load and context setup:
1. User opens classes.html with optional URL parameters (e.g., categoryId, query, filters, sortBy, page).
2. The page reads categoryId; if absent or invalid, the application uses its default listing category.
3. The page calls getAuthStatus to determine if wishlist actions should be enabled.
4. The page calls getClassListingFilterOptions with listingCategory derived from categoryId to retrieve available filter options for that context.
5. The page constructs a filters object from current URL parameters (level, audience, minAge, maxAge, dateRange, dayFilter, timeOfDay, locationZip, locationRadiusMiles, priceMax, ratingMin, classType, labels).
6. The page calls searchClassOfferings with listingCategory, query, filters, sortBy, page, and an application-defined pageSize.
7. The results list, summary, filters panel (with selected states), and compare bar (initialized using getWorkshopCompareState) are rendered based on responses.

Refining search with keyword and filters:
1. User enters or edits text in the keyword search field and submits the form.
2. The page updates the query URL parameter and resets the page URL parameter to the first page.
3. The page reconstructs the filters object from URL parameters and calls searchClassOfferings with the updated query.
4. Results list and summary update based on the new searchClassOfferings response.
5. User adjusts filters (e.g., level, audience, date range, day-of-week, time-of-day, location, price, rating, class type, labels) using controls populated from getClassListingFilterOptions.
6. Each filter change updates the corresponding URL parameter and resets page to the first page.
7. After each change, the page rebuilds the filters object and calls searchClassOfferings; results update accordingly.

Sorting and pagination:
1. User selects a sort option from the sort control.
2. The page updates the sortBy URL parameter and resets page to the first page.
3. The page calls searchClassOfferings with the new sortBy value; the results list updates accordingly.
4. User navigates between pages using pagination controls (e.g., next, previous, specific page numbers).
5. Pagination updates the page URL parameter while preserving categoryId, query, filters, and sortBy.
6. The page calls searchClassOfferings with the new page value; the new subset of results is rendered.
7. If the user uses the browser back/forward buttons, the page listens for URL changes, re-parses parameters, and re-runs searchClassOfferings to reflect the correct state.

Wishlist from class listings:
1. On load, the page has determined the authentication state via getAuthStatus.
2. For each class card where canAddToWishlist is true, a wishlist action is shown and enabled only when the user is authenticated.
3. User clicks the wishlist action on a class card.
4. If the user is not authenticated, the page shows a non-interfaced UI notice and does not call addClassOfferingToWishlist.
5. If authenticated, the page calls addClassOfferingToWishlist with the selected classOfferingId.
6. On success, the UI updates to indicate the item is saved (e.g., toggling icon state) and may update any wishlist count display based on totalWishlistItems returned.
7. If alreadyExisted is true, the UI shows a suitable non-interfaced informational message without changing wishlist backend state.

Workshop comparison from listings:
1. On page load or refresh, the page calls getWorkshopCompareState to obtain the current compare list and count.
2. For each class card with canAddToCompare true, a compare toggle control is displayed and its state reflects whether its offeringId is currently in the compare list.
3. User clicks the compare toggle on a workshop not in the list.
4. The page calls addWorkshopToCompare with the workshop’s classOfferingId.
5. Based on the response (offeringIds and count), the compare bar updates to show the selected workshops and enables or disables the compare action as appropriate (e.g., only active when a minimum required count is met).
6. User removes a workshop from comparison either via the card toggle or directly from the compare bar.
7. The page calls removeWorkshopFromCompare with that classOfferingId and updates the compare bar based on the response.
8. User may click a clear-all control in the compare bar.
9. The page calls clearWorkshopCompareList, then updates the compare bar to reflect an empty state.
10. When the compare bar shows a sufficient number of workshops for comparison, the user clicks the compare action, and the page navigates to compare.html according to the specification.

Opening class details from listings:
1. User clicks anywhere on a class card area designated for navigation (excluding wishlist or compare controls).
2. The page reads the associated offeringId from the result item returned by searchClassOfferings.
3. The page navigates to class_detail.html with id set to the clicked offeringId in the query string.
4. Browser history is updated so that returning to classes.html preserves URL parameters and therefore the previous search state.
interactions:
Keyword search form interactions:
- onChange (input): Update local form state without immediately changing URL.
- onSubmit: Write the entered value into the query URL parameter, reset page to the first page, and trigger a new searchClassOfferings call using the updated URL-derived parameters.

Filter control interactions (level, audience, age range, date range, day-of-week, time-of-day, location, price, rating, class type, labels):
- onChange: For each individual control, update the corresponding URL parameter (or remove it if cleared), reset page to the first page, then reconstruct the filters object from all URL parameters and call searchClassOfferings.
- onApply (for composite filter UI such as date range or location radius, if applicable): Batch-update multiple related URL parameters in a single history entry before triggering searchClassOfferings.

Sort control interactions:
- onChange: Update the sortBy URL parameter, reset page to the first page, and trigger searchClassOfferings to refresh results sorted according to the selection.

Pagination interactions:
- onClick (next/previous/specific page): Update the page URL parameter while preserving all other parameters, then call searchClassOfferings with the new page value.
- onPopState (browser back/forward): Re-parse all URL parameters and re-invoke searchClassOfferings and related initialization logic so that the UI reflects the navigated state.

Class card interactions:
- onClick (card body): Navigate to class_detail.html with id set to the card’s offeringId.
- onClick (wishlist control): If getAuthStatus indicates authenticated and canAddToWishlist is true, call addClassOfferingToWishlist with that offeringId and update the card’s wishlist state based on the response.
- onClick (compare toggle): If canAddToCompare is true, toggle between add/remove by checking current getWorkshopCompareState; call addWorkshopToCompare or removeWorkshopFromCompare accordingly, then refresh compare bar state.

Wishlist-specific interactions:
- onClick (wishlist icon when not authenticated): Show a UI message explaining that login is required; do not call addClassOfferingToWishlist.
- onSuccess (addClassOfferingToWishlist): Update any wishlist badge or indicator in the header or card using totalWishlistItems and alreadyExisted from the response.

Compare bar interactions:
- onInit: Call getWorkshopCompareState to populate initial offeringIds and count.
- onClick (remove item in bar): Call removeWorkshopFromCompare with that offeringId, then refresh local compare state with the response.
- onClick (clear all): Call clearWorkshopCompareList and reset compare bar display based on the returned empty state.
- onClick (compare action): Navigate to compare.html while retaining the current classes.html state in browser history.

Context/category navigation interactions:
- onClick (header or internal tabs to switch context, e.g., Classes, Online Classes, Kids & Teens, Workshops): Update the categoryId URL parameter to the chosen value and remove any filters that are no longer relevant for the new context if the application defines such rules, reset page to the first page, call getClassListingFilterOptions with the new listingCategory, and then call searchClassOfferings with parameters derived from the updated URL.
page_functionality.state_logic: The URL query string is the primary source of truth for the page state. On every load or history navigation, the page parses all defined URL parameters and reconstructs internal state and API request payloads accordingly. The categoryId parameter determines listingCategory passed to getClassListingFilterOptions and searchClassOfferings. If categoryId is missing or invalid according to application rules, a default listing category is used by the application configuration without being embedded as a static data set here. The query parameter represents the keyword search term and is passed directly to searchClassOfferings. Filter-related URL parameters (level, audience, minAge, maxAge, dateRange, dayFilter, timeOfDay, locationZip, locationRadiusMiles, priceMax, ratingMin, classType, labels) are read, normalized, and combined into the filters object passed to searchClassOfferings. The sortBy parameter controls the sorting behavior for searchClassOfferings. The page parameter represents the current pagination index; whenever any search, filter, or sort change occurs, the application resets page to the first page, updates the URL, and re-calls searchClassOfferings. Browser back/forward actions are handled by listening to URL changes; on any such event, the page re-runs initialization logic: it re-derives listingCategory from categoryId, re-calls getClassListingFilterOptions if needed, rebuilds the filters object from the updated URL, and calls searchClassOfferings. The authentication state is not stored in the URL; instead, getAuthStatus is called at load (and optionally on demand) and stored in memory for UI logic such as enabling wishlist actions. The workshop compare state is also not stored in the URL; it is obtained via getWorkshopCompareState on load and after any compare modification. All UI components derive their state from either the current URL parameters, the latest interface responses, or both, ensuring that reloading the page with a given URL reproduces the same listing, filter, and sort state.

components.functionality: Coordinates initial data loading and responds to URL changes by invoking getAuthStatus, getClassListingFilterOptions, getWorkshopCompareState, and searchClassOfferings with parameters derived from the current URL.
Displays the current listing context derived from categoryId and provides navigation controls to switch context, updating categoryId in the URL and triggering a full refresh of filters and results.
Provides an input for the user to enter or edit the keyword search term, synchronized with the query URL parameter, and triggers a new searchClassOfferings request when submitted.

Renders filter controls (level, audience, age, date range, day-of-week, time-of-day, location, price, rating, class type, labels) based on options returned by getClassListingFilterOptions and binds their selected values to the corresponding URL parameters used when calling searchClassOfferings.
Allows the user to select a sort order bound to sortBy in the URL and triggers searchClassOfferings with the chosen sort option.
Displays summary information such as total results and current filters context based on the latest searchClassOfferings response and the current URL parameters.

Renders a list or grid of class cards using the results array returned by searchClassOfferings, including title, pricing display, schedule summary, next available date, location summary, rating, and tags, and exposes navigation, wishlist, and compare actions per card.

Handles interactions with wishlist actions originating from class cards by checking authentication via getAuthStatus (on initialization) and calling addClassOfferingToWishlist when applicable, then updating local UI state based on the response.

Manages the selection state for workshop comparison, updating selection on card toggles via addWorkshopToCompare and removeWorkshopFromCompare, and synchronizing the compare bar with getWorkshopCompareState and clearWorkshopCompareList.
Displays currently selected workshops for comparison and provides actions to remove individual items, clear all, and navigate to compare.html when comparison is available.

Shows pagination controls derived from totalResults and pageSize from searchClassOfferings and the current page URL parameter, and updates page in the URL when the user navigates between result pages.

name: Class Detail
title: Class Detail
description: Detailed booking page for a specific art class, workshop, course, or kids’ class. Users can review full class information, select an available date/session, choose the number of participants and optional add-ons, then proceed to a booking summary with all selections passed via URL parameters.

core_features: Load and display detailed information for the selected ClassOffering (title, description, format, audience, level, location, pricing, rating, schedule summary) using getClassOfferingDetail with the id URL parameter.

Display upcoming single-session and multi-week course options for the selected class in a calendar/schedule selector, using getClassOfferingSchedulingOptions with configurable date ranges to support weekend, Friday, next-month, and evening discovery.
Allow selection of a specific ClassSession (for single_class, workshop, ongoing_program) or CourseInstance (for multi_week_course) as the chosen date/time or course start.
Let the user specify the number of participants for the booking, validating against available spots for the selected session when spotsRemaining data is available.

Load and manage selectable ClassAddon items (e.g., materials kit) using getClassOfferingAddons, allowing optional and required add-ons with quantity controls, and preparing an encoded add-on selection payload for navigation.

Provide primary booking CTAs whose label comes from bookingCtaLabel (e.g., "Book Now", "Book Workshop", "Book Course", "Enroll for Free"), and on click navigate to booking_summary.html with URL parameters for classOfferingId, selected classSessionId or courseInstanceId, participants, and encoded add-on selections.
Allow the user to add this class to their wishlist using addClassOfferingToWishlist (with access control based on getAuthStatus) and then navigate to wishlist.html via global navigation.

If the ClassOffering is a workshop, allow adding it to the comparison list using addWorkshopToCompare and show compare bar state using getWorkshopCompareState, with navigation to compare.html when the user chooses to compare.

user_workflows.name: View class details and understand the offering
steps: User arrives at class_detail.html with a valid id URL parameter.
Page-init logic reads id from the URL and calls getClassOfferingDetail(id).
While data is loading, the page shows a loading state; if the API fails or returns an inactive offering, the page shows a generic error state and disables booking-related components.

Once data is loaded, the Class Detail page displays title, subtitle, description, level, audience, age range label, offering format, location type, studio name, address, typical schedule information, pricing, rating, tags, and badges using fields from getClassOfferingDetail.
If the offeringFormat indicates a workshop, course, single class, or ongoing program, this is reflected in a format label and in the primary booking CTA label from bookingCtaLabel.

user_workflows.name: Select date/session or course start and time
steps: After class details load, the schedule selector component initializes a default date range (for example, starting from the nextAvailableDate or the current date) and calls getClassOfferingSchedulingOptions(classOfferingId, startDate, endDate).

The component displays available single sessions (sessions array) and course instances (courseInstances array) in a calendar and/or list, including weekday, time-of-day, weekend flags, and availability (spotsRemaining where provided).

User can use filters (e.g., weekend, Friday, evening, next month) which adjust the displayed options: weekend and evening are filtered client-side based on isWeekend and timeOfDay; next month or date navigation changes startDate and endDate and triggers another getClassOfferingSchedulingOptions call.
When user clicks on a specific session time slot, the component stores selectedClassSessionId in page state and clears any previously selectedCourseInstanceId.
When user selects a course instance (for multi_week_course), the component stores selectedCourseInstanceId in page state and clears any previously selectedClassSessionId.
The currently selected date/time or course run is highlighted and also reflected in the booking summary strip.

user_workflows.name: Set participants and choose add-ons
steps: User adjusts the number of participants via the participants selector. The component maintains selectedParticipants in page state with a minimum of 1.

If a ClassSession is selected and spotsRemaining is available, the participants selector prevents selecting a value greater than spotsRemaining; if the user tries to exceed it, the selector enforces the maximum and may show a non-hardcoded validation message.
In parallel, the page calls getClassOfferingAddons(classOfferingId).
The add-ons component lists all active add-ons (isActive true) with names, descriptions, prices, types, and quantity inputs.
If an add-on isRequired is true, the component ensures that a minimum quantity (for example 1) is enforced and cannot be deselected; optional add-ons can be toggled or set to quantity 0.
The add-ons component maintains an internal representation of selected add-ons (addonId and quantity), updates this as the user interacts, and exposes this selection to the booking summary component.
The booking summary strip updates any estimated pricing display based on basePrice, participants, and add-on totals (without hardcoded numeric values).

user_workflows.name: Complete booking and navigate to booking summary
steps: Once a valid combination of selections exists (classSessionId or courseInstanceId selected, participants >= 1, and all required add-ons satisfied), the primary booking CTA becomes enabled.
User clicks the primary booking CTA, which uses the bookingCtaLabel from getClassOfferingDetail as its text.

The click handler validates again: it checks that exactly one of selectedClassSessionId or selectedCourseInstanceId is set, that selectedParticipants is valid, and that all required add-ons have non-zero quantities.
The handler encodes add-on selections (each with addonId and quantity) into a URL-safe string (e.g., via JSON serialization and URL encoding) to use as the addons parameter.

The page then navigates the browser to booking_summary.html with URL parameters: classOfferingId set to the current id, classSessionId set to the selectedClassSessionId (or left empty if not applicable), courseInstanceId set to the selectedCourseInstanceId (or left empty if not applicable), participants set to selectedParticipants, and addons set to encodedAddonSelections.
The Booking Summary page will then reconstruct the booking context from these parameters.

user_workflows.name: Add class to wishlist
steps: On page init, the page calls getAuthStatus to determine if the user is authenticated.

If the user is authenticated, the wishlist button is interactive; if not, clicking it can trigger a non-hardcoded prompt to log in, without calling addClassOfferingToWishlist until the user is authenticated.
When the authenticated user clicks the wishlist button, the handler calls addClassOfferingToWishlist(classOfferingId).
The response indicates whether the item already existed in the wishlist via alreadyExisted; the component adjusts its UI (e.g., toggling to a saved state) according to this result.
The header’s wishlist icon (outside this page’s functional scope but part of global layout) can navigate to wishlist.html without additional parameters, showing the updated list.

user_workflows.name: Add workshop to compare and open comparison
steps: On page init, if the loaded offeringFormat is workshop, the page calls getWorkshopCompareState to determine current compare list.
The compare component shows whether this workshop is already in the compare list and how many workshops are currently selected based on the returned offeringIds and count.
When user clicks the compare action, the handler calls addWorkshopToCompare(classOfferingId).
The response returns the updated offeringIds and count plus a message field; the component updates its state to match the new compare list.
The page can expose a compare bar button that, when clicked, navigates to compare.html without additional parameters, where the comparison view uses the stored compare list on the backend.

interactions.behavior: Page reads id from the URL. If missing or empty, the page shows an error state and does not call data interfaces. If present, the page calls getAuthStatus and getClassOfferingDetail(id). Once the class detail is loaded and isActive is implied by the response, dependent components (schedule selector, add-ons, wishlist, compare) initialize using this classOfferingId.

interactions.trigger: User clicks controls such as next/previous week or month, or specific range filters (e.g., next month), in the schedule selector
interactions.behavior: Schedule selector calculates a new startDate and endDate and calls getClassOfferingSchedulingOptions(classOfferingId, startDate, endDate). It merges or replaces current schedule data, updates the visual calendar/list, and preserves the currently selected session or course instance if it still exists within the new data.

interactions.trigger: User toggles filter controls (e.g., weekend only, Friday only, evening only) in the schedule selector
interactions.behavior: The component does not call the API again but filters the existing sessions and courseInstances client-side using isWeekend, weekday, and timeOfDay fields. Only matching options are shown; selection remains valid if the selected option is still visible. If filtering hides the current selection, the component clears selectedClassSessionId/selectedCourseInstanceId and notifies the booking summary that no time is selected.

interactions.interaction: Select single session date and time
interactions.trigger: User clicks on a single ClassSession in the sessions list or calendar
interactions.behavior: The schedule selector sets selectedClassSessionId to the clicked session’s classSessionId and clears selectedCourseInstanceId. It also stores any metadata (startDateTime, endDateTime, weekday, timeOfDay, isWeekend, spotsRemaining) needed for display and validation. The participants selector may adjust its max value based on spotsRemaining; the booking summary updates its selected date/time summary.

interactions.trigger: User clicks on a CourseInstance option in the courseInstances list
interactions.behavior: The schedule selector sets selectedCourseInstanceId to the chosen courseInstanceId and clears selectedClassSessionId. It records associated fields (startDate, endDate, numberOfWeeks, sessionsPerWeek, scheduleDescription, timeOfDay, totalPrice, isEnrollmentOpen) for display and validation. If isEnrollmentOpen is false, the component prevents selection and shows a non-hardcoded message instead.

interactions.trigger: User increments or decrements participants in the participants selector
interactions.behavior: The participants selector updates selectedParticipants in page state, ensuring it is at least 1. If a session is selected and spotsRemaining is defined, the selector clamps the value to spotsRemaining. Any changes propagate to the booking summary for price estimation and validation.

interactions.trigger: User toggles add-on selection or changes quantities in the add-ons component
interactions.behavior: The add-ons component updates its internal selectedAddons structure (list of {addonId, quantity}). For required add-ons (isRequired true), it enforces minimum quantity > 0 and prevents deselection. It enforces maxQuantity if provided. It exposes the updated selection and computed add-on total to the booking summary strip.

interactions.trigger: User clicks the booking button labeled with bookingCtaLabel
interactions.behavior: The booking CTA handler validates that either selectedClassSessionId or selectedCourseInstanceId is set (but not both), that selectedParticipants is valid, and that all required add-ons have non-zero quantities. If validation passes, it encodes selected add-ons into a URL-safe string and updates the browser location to booking_summary.html with classOfferingId, classSessionId, courseInstanceId, participants, and addons as URL parameters.

interactions.trigger: User clicks the wishlist button or icon on the class detail page
interactions.behavior: If getAuthStatus indicates the user is authenticated, the handler calls addClassOfferingToWishlist(classOfferingId). Depending on the response alreadyExisted flag, the component updates to a saved state or indicates it was already saved, and may show the updated totalWishlistItems count. If the user is not authenticated, the component prompts for login without calling the wishlist API.

interactions.trigger: User clicks the compare action for a workshop-class offering
interactions.behavior: The handler calls addWorkshopToCompare(classOfferingId) and then updates the compare control state with the returned offeringIds and count. If the response message indicates capacity limits (e.g., maximum number of workshops already selected), the component shows this information using that message.

interactions.trigger: User clicks a "compare" bar/button once at least one workshop is in the compare list
interactions.behavior: The page navigates to compare.html without additional parameters; comparison data is resolved on that page using the server-side workshop compare state.

page_functionality.state_logic: The primary page state is derived from the URL id parameter and maintained in client-side variables without using localStorage. On load, the page reads id from the query string (e.g., class_detail.html?id=<value>). If id is missing or empty, the page enters an error state and does not call any data interfaces. When id is present, it becomes the classOfferingId used for all interface calls: getClassOfferingDetail(id), getClassOfferingSchedulingOptions(id, startDate, endDate) with date ranges determined by the schedule selector, and getClassOfferingAddons(id). The page maintains transient state for: (1) selectedClassSessionId and associated session metadata, (2) selectedCourseInstanceId and associated instance metadata, (3) selectedParticipants (default at least 1), (4) selectedAddons (list of {addonId, quantity}), (5) currentScheduleStartDate and currentScheduleEndDate, (6) schedule filters (weekend, specific weekdays like Friday, and timeOfDay such as evening), (7) wishlist state (derived from addClassOfferingToWishlist responses during this session) and (8) compare state (initialized via getWorkshopCompareState and updated via addWorkshopToCompare). These states affect which components are enabled, which CTA labels and summaries are shown, and whether booking is allowed. When the user clicks the primary booking CTA, the page constructs a URL for booking_summary.html using the original id as classOfferingId and including the current selectedClassSessionId (if a single session booking), selectedCourseInstanceId (if a course instance booking), selectedParticipants, and an addons parameter. The addons parameter is an encoded representation (e.g., JSON string that is URL-encoded) of the selectedAddons structure so the Booking Summary page can reconstruct the user’s add-on choices. No state is persisted in localStorage; any cross-page persistence beyond the immediate navigation is handled by the backend through the assigned interfaces or by reconstructing state from URL parameters on the target page.

components.functionality: Reads the id URL parameter, validates it, triggers initial API calls to getAuthStatus and getClassOfferingDetail, and then initializes dependent components with the loaded ClassOffering data.

Displays core information for the ClassOffering including title, subtitle, categoryName, level, audience, ageRangeLabel, offeringFormat, pricing (basePrice and priceDisplay), ratingAverage and ratingCount, durationMinutes, numberOfSessions, durationWeeks, primaryWeekday, typicalStartTime, typicalTimeOfDay, tags, labelBadges, and nextAvailableDate. It also shows imageUrl when available and uses bookingCtaLabel to inform the primary CTA text.

Shows location-related and meta information such as locationType (in_person or online), studioName, fullAddress, audience, age range, and any trial/intro flags (isTrial, isIntro). It also provides context text for online versus in-person attendance and highlights labelBadges as keywords.

Allows the user to browse and select available dates and times for the class by combining calendar navigation and a list of options using sessions and courseInstances returned from getClassOfferingSchedulingOptions. It supports date-range navigation (e.g., current period, next month), and client-side filters for weekend, specific weekdays such as Friday, and time-of-day (morning, afternoon, evening). It manages the selection of either a single ClassSession (setting selectedClassSessionId) or a CourseInstance (setting selectedCourseInstanceId) and ensures only one of these is selected at a time.

Provides controls to set the number of participants for the booking. Maintains selectedParticipants state starting from 1 and adjusts its maximum based on the spotsRemaining for the currently selected ClassSession when available. It notifies the booking summary when participants change.

Fetches active add-ons for the class using getClassOfferingAddons and renders each add-on with its name, description, price, type, required flag, and quantity controls. Enforces that required add-ons (isRequired) have at least a minimum quantity and optional add-ons can be deselected. Observes maxQuantity where provided. Maintains a selectedAddons list (addonId and quantity) and exposes it to other components for price estimation and URL encoding.

Allows logged-in users to add the current ClassOffering to their wishlist. Uses getAuthStatus to determine if the button should be active. When clicked and authenticated, it calls addClassOfferingToWishlist(classOfferingId), updates its state based on alreadyExisted, and may update a displayed total wishlist count using totalWishlistItems. If not authenticated, it triggers a login prompt and does not call the API.

Visible when the loaded ClassOffering has offeringFormat set to workshop. On init, calls getWorkshopCompareState to determine which workshops are already in the comparison list. On user click, calls addWorkshopToCompare(classOfferingId) to add this workshop. Updates its UI to reflect the new offeringIds and count and can display the message from the API (for example, indicating limits). Exposes a control to navigate to compare.html when the user chooses to view their selected workshops.

Summarizes the user’s selection (chosen date/session or course instance, participants count, and selected add-ons) and presents the primary booking CTA using bookingCtaLabel from getClassOfferingDetail. It validates that a session or course instance is selected, participants is valid, and required add-ons are selected. On CTA click, it constructs the navigation URL to booking_summary.html with query parameters classOfferingId, classSessionId, courseInstanceId, participants, and addons (encoded from selected add-ons) and triggers navigation.

Manages display of global loading and error states for this page. Shows a loading indicator while getClassOfferingDetail is pending, and conditionally while scheduling options and add-ons are being fetched. Shows non-hardcoded error messages if any of the core interfaces fail or if the classOfferingId is invalid, and disables booking-related interactions until resolved.

name: Booking Summary / Enrollment
title: Booking Summary and Enrollment
description: This page summarizes the selected class or course booking, shows the pricing breakdown, collects attendee details and payment method, and confirms the booking using the provided selection and user input.

core_features: Load a booking summary using getBookingSummaryForSelection based on URL parameters (classOfferingId, classSessionId or courseInstanceId, participants, addons decoded to selectedAddons).
Display key booking details: class title, booking type, date/time label, location summary, and number of participants, with a link to change date/time via navigation to class_detail.html.
Allow the participant count to be adjusted on this page, updating the participants URL parameter and reloading the booking summary via getBookingSummaryForSelection.
Present a read-only summary of selected add-ons with quantities and line totals, using the selectedAddons data returned by getBookingSummaryForSelection.
Show a pricing breakdown (base price per participant, base subtotal, add-on total, overall total, currency), clearly handling both paid and free (isFree or zero total) bookings.
Collect attendee information (name and email) in a validated form prior to confirmation.
Offer only the payment options provided by bookingSummary.availablePaymentMethods and handle special cases such as no payment required for free bookings.
Submit the final booking via confirmBooking using the selection parameters, attendee info, selected add-ons, and chosen payment method.
Display booking confirmation details and a confirmation message from confirmBooking, and offer navigation to my_bookings.html after successful confirmation.
Handle invalid or missing URL parameters by preventing API calls, showing a functional error state, and providing navigation back to class_detail.html.
user_workflows: Workflow: Paid single class or workshop booking — 1) User arrives from class_detail.html with classOfferingId, classSessionId, participants, addons in the URL. 2) Page parses URL parameters, decodes addons into selectedAddons, and calls getBookingSummaryForSelection. 3) User reviews the booking details and pricing. 4) User optionally adjusts the number of participants; the page updates the participants URL parameter and re-fetches the summary. 5) User fills in attendee name and email. 6) User selects one of the available payment methods (for example, pay at studio or pay now, depending on availablePaymentMethods). 7) User submits the confirmation form, triggering confirmBooking with current selection, attendee info, selectedAddons, and paymentMethod. 8) On success, the page displays the confirmation message and booking details and offers a link to my_bookings.html.

Workflow: Free trial or free intro class enrollment — 1) User arrives from class_detail.html with selection URL parameters as above. 2) The page calls getBookingSummaryForSelection, which returns isFree and a totalPrice of zero. 3) The pricing panel clearly shows that total payment due is zero. 4) If availablePaymentMethods includes a no-payment-required method (for example, none_required), the payment selector defaults to that and may hide unnecessary payment inputs. 5) User enters attendee name and email. 6) User confirms the enrollment, calling confirmBooking with paymentMethod determined by availablePaymentMethods. 7) Page shows the confirmation message and details and provides a link to my_bookings.html.

Workflow: Multi-week course enrollment — 1) User arrives with classOfferingId and courseInstanceId (and optionally addons and participants) in the URL. 2) The page calls getBookingSummaryForSelection; the backend determines bookingType and schedule based on courseInstanceId. 3) User reviews course dates, schedule description (via dateLabel and time information), and pricing. 4) User may adjust participants, triggering URL update and summary reload. 5) User enters attendee information and selects a payment method from availablePaymentMethods (for example, pay at first session or pay in full now). 6) User submits the confirmation form (confirmBooking). 7) Page shows confirmation and a link to my_bookings.html.

Workflow: Changing schedule before confirmation — 1) User on this page decides to change the selected date or course instance. 2) User clicks the 'Change date/time' link in the booking details summary. 3) Browser navigates to class_detail.html with id set to the current classOfferingId, allowing the user to pick a new session or course instance. 4) After changing the schedule on class_detail.html, the user proceeds again to booking_summary.html with updated URL parameters, and the flow restarts with a fresh getBookingSummaryForSelection call.
interactions: On initial page load or any URL parameter change, the page parses classOfferingId, classSessionId, courseInstanceId, participants, and addons from the query string, transforms addons into selectedAddons, and calls getBookingSummaryForSelection. While loading, a loading state is shown; on error (such as missing required parameters), an error state is shown.

When the user clicks the 'Change date/time' link in the booking details section, the page performs a navigation to class_detail.html?id={classOfferingId} using the current classOfferingId from the URL or summary data.

When the user changes the participants count via an input control, the component updates the participants value in the URL query string (e.g., via a navigation or history update to booking_summary.html with the new participants parameter) and then re-runs getBookingSummaryForSelection with the updated participants while preserving the other selection parameters and selectedAddons.

When the booking summary is successfully loaded from getBookingSummaryForSelection, the details, add-ons, and pricing components bind to the returned values (classTitle, bookingType, dateLabel, locationSummary, participants, pricing, selectedAddons) and render them in read-only form.

When the user fills out the attendee information fields (name and email), the form component updates its internal state and performs validation (such as required and email format) before allowing submission.

When the user selects a payment method option, the payment-method-selector component updates the chosen paymentMethod in form state, limited strictly to values present in availablePaymentMethods from getBookingSummaryForSelection. If isFree is true and an appropriate no-payment-required method is available, the selector may auto-select it and hide additional payment-related instructions.

When the user clicks the main confirmation button (such as 'Confirm Booking' or 'Confirm Enrollment'), the booking-confirmation-form validates attendee info and paymentMethod, and if valid, calls confirmBooking with classOfferingId, classSessionId, courseInstanceId, participants, selectedAddons, attendeeName, attendeeEmail, and paymentMethod from the current state.

When confirmBooking succeeds, the page switches from the input form view to a confirmation view, using confirmationMessage, classTitle, dateLabel, startDateTime, locationSummary, participants, totalPrice, currency, bookingStatus, and paymentStatus from the confirmBooking response. The confirmation view also exposes a link or button to navigate to my_bookings.html, and the confirmation form is disabled or hidden to prevent duplicate submission.

If confirmBooking returns an error (for example, due to validation or availability issues), the booking-confirmation-form displays an error message, keeps the existing form values, and allows the user to attempt submission again after resolving issues.
If the user clicks a 'View My Bookings' link or button after confirmation, the browser navigates directly to my_bookings.html without additional API calls from this page.
page_functionality.state_logic: The page uses query string URL parameters as the single source of truth for the selected class or course and the number of participants. On initial load, it reads classOfferingId (required) and optionally classSessionId and/or courseInstanceId; these are passed through to getBookingSummaryForSelection and later confirmBooking. The participants parameter is also read from the URL and determines the initial participant count in the summary and pricing. The addons URL parameter is an encoded representation of the selected add-ons; the page decodes this into a selectedAddons array that is passed into getBookingSummaryForSelection and, unchanged unless explicitly updated, into confirmBooking. Any change to participants on this page updates the participants query parameter (for example, by pushing or replacing a URL like booking_summary.html?classOfferingId=...&classSessionId=...&participants=...) and triggers a fresh call to getBookingSummaryForSelection with the new participants while retaining other selection parameters from the URL and the decoded selectedAddons. The presence of classSessionId or courseInstanceId in the URL determines whether the booking is for a single class/workshop or a multi-week course; both can be forwarded to getBookingSummaryForSelection, and the bookingType field in the response dictates how the UI labels and interprets the booking. Internal UI state (such as in-progress attendee form values and the currently chosen paymentMethod) is kept in component state but does not alter the URL until a booking is confirmed. After confirmBooking succeeds, the page holds the returned booking data in component state and swaps to a confirmation view; optionally, the browser can update the URL to include a bookingId or a confirmation flag using history replacement, but the functional behavior of the page does not depend on any additional URL parameters beyond those required for the initial summary. No localStorage or other persistent client-side storage is used; all state relevant to the selection comes from the current URL and live API responses.

components.functionality: Parses URL parameters (classOfferingId, classSessionId, courseInstanceId, participants, addons), transforms the addons string into a selectedAddons array, invokes getBookingSummaryForSelection with these values, and exposes the resulting summary data, loading state, and error state to child components.

Displays the core booking information from the summary, including classTitle, bookingType, dateLabel, formatted startDateTime and endDateTime (if present), participants count, and locationSummary. Provides an interactive participants control that, when changed, updates the participants URL parameter and triggers a re-fetch of the summary. Includes a 'Change date/time' link that navigates back to class_detail.html with the current classOfferingId.

Shows a read-only list of selected add-ons from the booking summary. For each item in selectedAddons, it displays name, quantity, unitPrice, and lineTotal, relying entirely on the selectedAddons array returned by getBookingSummaryForSelection.

Displays the pricing breakdown using the pricing object and isFree flag from the booking summary. Shows basePricePerParticipant, baseSubtotal, addOnTotal, totalPrice, and currency, and adjusts labels or messaging when isFree is true or when totalPrice is zero to indicate that no payment is required.

Collects attendeeName and attendeeEmail from the user. Performs basic validation (such as required fields and email format) and exposes the validated values to the booking-confirmation-form for inclusion in the confirmBooking call.

Presents a list of payment options derived from availablePaymentMethods in the booking summary. Restricts selection strictly to the methods provided by the API and updates the selected paymentMethod in the parent form state. If isFree is true and a no-payment-required method is provided, the component can auto-select that option and optionally hide or simplify the control.

Coordinates the attendee-info-form and payment-method-selector to assemble all necessary data to confirm the booking. On submission, validates attendeeName, attendeeEmail, and paymentMethod, then calls confirmBooking with the current selection (classOfferingId, classSessionId, courseInstanceId, participants from URL and state, selectedAddons decoded from URL), plus the attendee info and chosen paymentMethod. Manages loading and error states during the confirmBooking call and passes successful confirmation data to the booking-confirmation-panel.

Displays the outcome of a successful confirmBooking call. Shows confirmationMessage, classTitle, dateLabel, startDateTime, locationSummary, participants, totalPrice, currency, bookingStatus, and paymentStatus from the confirmation response. Replaces or disables the input form to prevent duplicate submissions and exposes navigation controls to my_bookings.html and optionally to start a new booking via navigation back to class_detail.html.

name: Passes & Packages
title: Passes & Packages
description: Page for searching, filtering, and browsing multi-class pass and package products, with navigation to detailed pass information for purchase.

core_features: Load available filter options (categories, location types, time-of-day restrictions, number-of-classes options, price ranges) using the getPassFilterOptions interface and bind them to filter controls.
Derive the current search and filter state from URL parameters (query, categoryId, locationType, timeOfDayRestriction, numClassesIncluded, priceMax, sortBy).
Execute pass search requests using the searchPassProducts interface whenever the URL-derived state changes, and display pass/package results.
Provide a keyword search form that updates the query URL parameter and triggers a new search.
Provide filter controls (category, location type, time of day, number of classes, maximum price) that update corresponding URL parameters and re-run the search.
Provide a sort control that updates the sortBy URL parameter and re-runs the search.

Display pass/package summary cards populated from searchPassProducts results, including name, category, location type, time-of-day restriction, number of included classes, price, and validity period when available.
Allow users to navigate from a pass/package card to the pass detail page (pass_detail.html) using the passProductId in the URL.
user_workflows: 1) Page load with optional deep-link filters: a) Browser loads passes.html with zero or more URL parameters (query, categoryId, locationType, timeOfDayRestriction, numClassesIncluded, priceMax, sortBy). b) The page calls getPassFilterOptions to populate all filter controls. c) The page reads the URL parameters, initializes the search form and filter controls to reflect the URL state, and determines the active sort option. d) The page builds a filters object from the URL parameters and calls searchPassProducts with the query, filters, and sortBy values. e) Results from searchPassProducts are rendered as pass cards; loading and empty states are shown as needed.

2) Searching for passes by keyword: a) User types a keyword into the search input. b) User submits the search form (e.g., presses Enter or clicks a search button). c) The page updates the query URL parameter to match the entered keyword while preserving any existing filter and sort URL parameters. d) The page triggers searchPassProducts with the updated query and current filter/sort values. e) The result list updates to display only passes whose metadata matches the keyword.

3) Refining passes using filters: a) User adjusts one or more filters (e.g., selects a category, changes location type, chooses a time-of-day restriction, modifies the number-of-classes filter, or sets a maximum price). b) For each change, the page updates the corresponding URL parameter (categoryId, locationType, timeOfDayRestriction, numClassesIncluded, priceMax), removing parameters if filters are cleared. c) The page builds the updated filters object from the URL and calls searchPassProducts with the current query and sortBy values. d) The results list updates to show passes that satisfy all active filters.

4) Sorting passes: a) User chooses a sort option from a sort control that exposes supported sortBy values (for example, by price or by value). b) The page updates the sortBy URL parameter. c) The page calls searchPassProducts with the existing query and filter parameters, plus the new sortBy value. d) The pass cards are re-rendered according to the chosen sort order.

5) Navigating to a pass detail page: a) User identifies a pass/package of interest in the results list. b) User clicks the corresponding pass card or its dedicated detail-action element. c) The page navigates to pass_detail.html with an id URL parameter set to the clicked pass's passProductId. d) The user completes purchase or further exploration on the pass detail page.

6) Using browser navigation (back/forward) with state restoration: a) User performs several searches and filter changes, which update the URL each time. b) User clicks the browser Back or Forward button. c) The page detects the URL state change (e.g., via popstate) and re-initializes the search form and filters to match the new URL parameters. d) The page re-calls searchPassProducts with parameters derived from the updated URL, restoring the corresponding results.

7) Clearing all filters and search: a) User clicks a dedicated 'clear' action within the filters/search area. b) The page removes query, categoryId, locationType, timeOfDayRestriction, numClassesIncluded, priceMax, and sortBy from the URL or resets them to defaults. c) The search form and filter controls are reset to their baseline state. d) The page calls searchPassProducts with no filters and the default sort behavior. e) The result list updates to show all active passes that the user is allowed to see.
interactions: User types text into the keyword search input; the component updates internal state but does not query until onSubmit.
User submits the search form; the page updates the query URL parameter, preserves other parameters, and calls searchPassProducts.
User selects or deselects a category option; the page updates the categoryId URL parameter (or removes it when cleared) and calls searchPassProducts.
User selects a location type filter; the page sets or clears the locationType URL parameter and calls searchPassProducts.
User selects a time-of-day restriction filter; the page sets or clears the timeOfDayRestriction URL parameter and calls searchPassProducts.
User selects a number-of-classes option; the page sets or clears the numClassesIncluded URL parameter and calls searchPassProducts.
User adjusts a maximum price filter (e.g., via slider or list option) and then applies it; the page sets or clears the priceMax URL parameter and calls searchPassProducts.
User selects a sort option; the page updates the sortBy URL parameter and calls searchPassProducts.
User clicks on a pass card; the page reads that card's passProductId and navigates to pass_detail.html?id={passProductId}.
User clicks a 'clear filters' or 'reset' action; the page removes all filter-related URL parameters and re-runs searchPassProducts.
User uses browser Back/Forward; the page responds to the URL change by updating control states and re-calling searchPassProducts.
page_functionality.state_logic: The page's primary source of truth for search and filter state is the URL query string. On initialization, the page reads query, categoryId, locationType, timeOfDayRestriction, numClassesIncluded, priceMax, and sortBy from the URL (if present). It then initializes internal state and UI control values to mirror these parameters. Any user interaction that changes search input, filters, or sort order triggers a URL update using pushState or replaceState, ensuring that the URL always reflects the current state. After every URL state update, the page constructs a filters object: it includes categoryId when present, includes locationType when present, includes timeOfDayRestriction when present, includes numClassesIncluded when present, and includes a maximum price derived from priceMax when present. This filters object, along with the query parameter and the sortBy parameter (when present), is passed into searchPassProducts. If a parameter is absent or cleared, that filter is omitted from the filters object so the backend can apply its default behavior. The getPassFilterOptions interface is called once on or shortly after page load, independent of the current URL, and its results are used to populate the filter controls. When the browser navigation state changes (e.g., via back/forward), the page re-reads the URL parameters, updates control states, reconstructs the filters object, and calls searchPassProducts again, keeping UI, URL, and data results consistent without relying on localStorage or other persistent client-side storage.

components.functionality: Synchronizes the page's internal search and filter state with URL parameters and triggers pass searches when the URL changes.
data_binding: URLParameters(query, categoryId, locationType, timeOfDayRestriction, numClassesIncluded, priceMax, sortBy)

components.functionality: Loads available filter options for categories, location types, time-of-day restrictions, number-of-classes options, and price ranges, and exposes them to filter UI components.
Provides a keyword input for searching passes by name, description, category, or other indexed fields and updates the query URL parameter when submitted.
Displays and manages filters for category, location type, time-of-day restriction, number of included classes, and maximum price, updating URL parameters and triggering new searches on change.
Allows the user to choose the sort order of pass results and updates the sortBy URL parameter accordingly.
Displays a summary of the current result set (e.g., count of passes returned) and the active filters derived from URL parameters.
data_binding: URLParameters(query, categoryId, locationType, timeOfDayRestriction, numClassesIncluded, priceMax, sortBy)

components.functionality: Renders the list of pass/package products returned by searchPassProducts as a collection of pass cards and handles loading and empty states.
Displays a single pass/package summary and provides navigation to the pass detail page when clicked.
Handles navigation to the pass detail page using the selected pass's passProductId.
Shows or hides a loading state while searchPassProducts or getPassFilterOptions requests are in progress.
Displays a message and optional guidance when searchPassProducts returns no results for the current filters.
data_binding: URLParameters(query, categoryId, locationType, timeOfDayRestriction, numClassesIncluded, priceMax)

components.functionality: Displays error information when calls to getPassFilterOptions or searchPassProducts fail and optionally offers a retry action.

name: Pass / Package Detail
title: Pass / Package Detail
description: Displays details for a selected art class pass or package, lets the user choose a pass start date and quantity, and then navigates to checkout with those selections.

core_features: Read the pass or package ID from the URL query parameter `id` and use it to call `getPassProductDetail`.

Display pass/package details from `getPassProductDetail`, including name, description, category name, location type, time-of-day restrictions, number of classes included, price, validity period, terms summary, and eligible class types.
Provide a date picker for selecting a pass start date, constrained to today or later and within the next 30 days from the current date, with validation and error messaging.
Provide a quantity selector for the number of passes, defaulting to 1 and validating that the quantity is at least 1.
Show a dynamic purchase summary using the unit price from `getPassProductDetail` and the selected quantity (for display only).

Enable a 'Buy Package' action that validates inputs and, on success, navigates to `checkout.html` with URL parameters: `orderType=pass`, `passProductId` (from `id`), `quantity` (from user selection), and `startDate` (from date picker).
Reflect key user selections (start date and quantity) in the page URL as query parameters so that the state can be restored on reload or share.
user_workflows:
Workflow: View pass details and buy a package
1. User navigates from `passes.html` to `pass_detail.html?id={passProductId}`.
2. On page load, the page reads the `id` URL parameter.
3. The page calls `getPassProductDetail` with `passProductId = id` and shows a loading state.
4. When data is returned, the page displays the pass name, description, category name, time-of-day restriction, location type, number of classes included, price, validity days, terms summary, and eligible class types.
5. The user selects a start date using the date picker (limited to today through 30 days from today). The page validates the date and, if valid, clears any related error message.
6. The user adjusts the quantity (default 1) using the quantity selector; the page validates that the quantity is at least 1 and updates any validation message.
7. As the user changes quantity, the page updates a computed total cost display using the unit price from `getPassProductDetail`.
8. The user clicks the 'Buy Package' button.
9. The page validates that a valid start date is selected and that quantity is valid. If validation fails, it shows inline error messages and does not navigate.
10. If validation passes, the page navigates to `checkout.html` with URL parameters `orderType=pass`, `passProductId={id}`, `quantity={selectedQuantity}`, and `startDate={selectedStartDate}`, where `startDate` is serialized (e.g., `YYYY-MM-DD`).

Workflow: Restore selections from URL and continue to checkout
1. User lands on `pass_detail.html?id={passProductId}&quantity={q}&startDate={d}` via a shared or bookmarked link.
2. On load, the page reads `id`, `quantity`, and `startDate` from the URL query string.
3. The page calls `getPassProductDetail` with `passProductId = id`.
4. When data is returned, the page pre-populates the quantity selector with `quantity` (if it parses to a valid number ≥ 1) and the date picker with `startDate` (if it parses as a valid date within the allowed range of today to today + 30 days). Invalid URL values are ignored and replaced with defaults.
5. The user can adjust start date and quantity as needed; the URL is updated to reflect new values.
6. The user clicks 'Buy Package' and is navigated to `checkout.html` with parameters derived from the current selections.

Workflow: Handle errors when loading or validating
1. User opens `pass_detail.html` without an `id` URL parameter, or with an invalid `id`.
2. The page detects the missing or invalid `id` before or after calling `getPassProductDetail`.
3. If `id` is missing, the page shows an error banner indicating that the pass cannot be found and disables purchase controls.
4. If `getPassProductDetail` fails or returns no usable data, the page shows an error banner and a link/navigation control to return to `passes.html`. The 'Buy Package' button remains disabled.
5. If the user selects an invalid start date (e.g., a date before today or beyond 30 days from today) or a quantity less than 1, the page displays in-place validation messages and prevents navigation to checkout until corrected.
interactions: On page load: Read `id`, `quantity`, and `startDate` from the URL query string. If `id` is present, call `getPassProductDetail({ passProductId: id })`. Initialize internal state for loading, error, pass details, selected date, and quantity.

On data load success: Store the `getPassProductDetail` response in page state and render all detail components. Hide loading indicators and any prior error related to data fetching. Ensure 'Buy Package' button enabled only when required fields are valid.
On data load error: Set an error state, show an error banner with retry/back actions, and disable the 'Buy Package' button.

On date picker change: Update the selected start date in page state. Validate that the date is today or later and no more than 30 days from today. If valid, clear date-related error and update the `startDate` URL parameter (e.g., using `history.replaceState`). If invalid, show a validation message and keep the 'Buy Package' action disabled until corrected.

On quantity input change: Parse the input as a number. If it is at least 1, update the quantity in page state, clear quantity-related errors, and update the `quantity` URL parameter. If it is invalid or less than 1, show a validation error and disable the 'Buy Package' button.

On 'Buy Package' button click: Run client-side validation for both start date and quantity. If either is invalid or missing, show inline error messages and do not navigate. If valid, construct the checkout navigation URL `checkout.html?orderType=pass&passProductId={id}&quantity={selectedQuantity}&startDate={selectedStartDate}` and navigate.
On error banner 'Retry' click: Re-call `getPassProductDetail` with the current `id` parameter, reset loading and error state, and re-render results.
On 'Back to passes' navigation click: Navigate to `passes.html` (no additional logic required on this page).
page_functionality.state_logic: The primary state driver is the URL query string. The `id` parameter is required and is used to call `getPassProductDetail(passProductId = id)`. When `id` is missing or empty, the page enters an error state and disables purchase actions. Optional URL parameters `quantity` and `startDate` are used to initialize user selections. On load, if `quantity` is present and parses to a number ≥ 1, it is used as the initial quantity; otherwise the page defaults to a quantity of 1 and updates the URL accordingly. If `startDate` is present and parses as a valid date within the allowed window (today through today + 30 days), it is used as the initial selected start date; otherwise, no date is selected by default and date validation will require the user to pick one. As the user changes quantity or start date, the page updates these values in the URL query string (e.g., via `history.replaceState`) so that reloading the page restores the same state. No selection state is stored in localStorage or other persistent client storage; all restorable state is encoded in URL parameters. Internal UI states such as loading, error flags, and inline validation messages are ephemeral and reset on page reload.

components.functionality: Reads the `id` URL parameter, calls `getPassProductDetail` with `passProductId = id`, and manages loading and error states for the rest of the page.
event_handlers: onInit (triggered on page load to parse URL and call getPassProductDetail)
onRetryClick (re-calls getPassProductDetail if a previous fetch failed)

components.functionality: Displays key pass/package identifiers such as name, category name, and price display once data has loaded. Also shows a minimal navigation control back to the passes listing.
event_handlers: onBackClick (navigates to passes.html when the user clicks a back control)
onDataChange (updates displayed fields when new pass data is loaded)

components.functionality: Summarizes how the pass can be used, including number of included classes, eligible locations, time-of-day restrictions, and eligible class types.
event_handlers: onDataChange (re-renders usage information when pass details change)

components.functionality: Displays the validity period of the pass and any summarized terms or restrictions for user reference.
event_handlers: onDataChange (updates terms and validity when pass details change)

components.functionality: Provides a date picker for the user to select the pass start date, enforcing that the selected date is not in the past and is within the next 30 days.
data_binding: PassProduct (for display context only, not for validation)
event_handlers: onInit (initializes the field from URLParameters.startDate if valid)
onChange (updates PageState.selectedStartDate and URLParameters.startDate and performs validation)
onValidate (runs when the field is changed or on form submit to ensure the date is within allowed range)

components.functionality: Allows the user to set the quantity of passes to purchase, defaulting to 1 and ensuring the value stays at or above 1.
event_handlers: onInit (reads URLParameters.quantity and sets PageState.selectedQuantity if valid, otherwise defaults to 1 and updates URL)
onChange (updates PageState.selectedQuantity and URLParameters.quantity, validates that quantity ≥ 1)
onValidate (runs when the field is changed or on form submit to ensure an acceptable quantity)

components.functionality: Shows a summary of the user’s current selections, including selected start date, selected quantity, and a computed estimated total cost using the unit price from the pass details.
event_handlers: onQuantityChange (recalculates and re-renders the estimated total when quantity changes)
onDateChange (updates the displayed start date when it changes)
