components.functionality: Coordinates the start date selector, quantity selector, and 'Buy Package' button. Performs final validation and, if valid, constructs the checkout URL and navigates to `checkout.html` with the required parameters.
event_handlers: onSubmit (validates start date and quantity; on success, navigates to `checkout.html?orderType=pass&passProductId={id}&quantity={selectedQuantity}&startDate={selectedStartDate}`)
onValidationError (displays inline error messages and prevents navigation)
onInputChange (listens to child field changes to keep form-level validity state updated)

components.functionality: Displays errors related to missing `id` parameter, failed `getPassProductDetail` calls, or global validation failures that prevent purchasing.
event_handlers: onRetryClick (if the error is fetch-related, triggers pass-detail-data-loader.onRetryClick)
onDismiss (hides non-critical messages while keeping state intact)

name: Gift Cards
title: Gift Cards
description: Configure and purchase gift cards for art classes, including amount selection, delivery method, personalization, and adding the configured gift card to the shopping cart.

core_features: Load available gift card products and supported delivery methods using getGiftCardOptions, and expose them to the page for selection.
Allow the user to select a specific gift card product, then choose either a fixed amount or enter a custom amount according to the product’s configuration rules.
Allow the user to choose a delivery method (including printable vouchers) from the supported delivery methods returned by getGiftCardOptions.
Collect personalization details including recipient name and an optional message for the selected gift card configuration.

Continuously validate the current gift card configuration (amount, delivery method, recipient, message, quantity) using getGiftCardConfigurationSummary and display a summary of the configured gift card, including any validation errors.

Enable an Add to Cart action that calls addConfiguredGiftCardToCart to create or update the user’s cart with the configured gift card, and update cart-related UI using the returned cartItemCount and cartSubtotal.
Provide navigation controls to view the cart (cart.html) or proceed directly to checkout (checkout.html) after adding gift cards to the cart.
user_workflows:
Workflow 1: Configure and add a standard gift card to cart
1) User opens gift_cards.html (optionally with URL parameters).
2) Page initialization calls getGiftCardOptions with no parameters.
3) The page selects an initial gift card product based on URL parameters (giftCardProductId) if valid, otherwise the first active product from giftCardProducts.
4) The product selector displays all available giftCardProducts and highlights the current selection.
5) For the selected product, the amount selector shows its fixedAmounts and, if allowsCustomAmount is true, a custom amount input constrained by minCustomAmount and maxCustomAmount.
6) The user chooses a fixed amount or enters a custom amount.
7) The user selects a delivery method from supportedDeliveryMethods (ensuring printable_voucher is available when supported).
8) The user enters recipientName and an optional message, and adjusts quantity if needed.
9) On each relevant change (product, amount, deliveryMethod, recipientName, message, quantity), the controller calls getGiftCardConfigurationSummary with the current configuration.
10) The summary panel updates to show summaryLine, amount, currency, deliveryMethod, recipientName, message, quantity, or displays validationError if isValid is false.
11) Once getGiftCardConfigurationSummary returns isValid = true, the Add to Cart button becomes enabled.
12) The user clicks Add to Cart, triggering addConfiguredGiftCardToCart with the same configuration parameters.
13) On success, the page updates cart indicators using cartItemCount and cartSubtotal and shows a confirmation message; the user can then click a link/button to navigate to cart.html or click Checkout now to navigate to checkout.html.

Workflow 2: Open a pre-configured gift card link
1) User opens a URL like gift_cards.html with query parameters (e.g., giftCardProductId, amount, deliveryMethod, recipientName, message, quantity) provided by a sharer or previous session.
2) Page initialization calls getGiftCardOptions.
3) The controller parses URL parameters and attempts to match giftCardProductId with one of the returned giftCardProducts; if not found or inactive, it falls back to a default product and ignores the invalid ID.
4) If a valid product is found, the controller pre-populates the selected product, amount, deliveryMethod, recipientName, message, and quantity fields from the URL parameters, applying only values that are compatible with the product configuration (for example, only applying amount if it matches one of the fixedAmounts or falls within minCustomAmount and maxCustomAmount).
5) After pre-population, the controller calls getGiftCardConfigurationSummary once with the reconstructed configuration.
6) The summary panel shows the configuration summary or a validationError indicating what must be corrected.
7) The user can adjust any field as needed; URL parameters are updated accordingly (without reloading the page) so that the configuration remains shareable.
8) Once the configuration becomes valid, the user proceeds with Add to Cart, which calls addConfiguredGiftCardToCart, then navigates to cart.html or checkout.html if desired.

Workflow 3: Handling validation errors while configuring a gift card
1) User selects a gift card product and attempts to enter an amount or other details that do not meet the product’s rules (for example, an amount outside minCustomAmount/maxCustomAmount, an unsupported deliveryMethod, or an invalid quantity).
2) On field change or blur, the controller calls getGiftCardConfigurationSummary with the current configuration.
3) getGiftCardConfigurationSummary returns isValid = false and a validationError string.
4) The summary panel displays validationError clearly and marks affected fields via component-level validation (e.g., inline messages) without adding the item to the cart.
5) The Add to Cart button remains disabled while isValid is false.
6) The user corrects the configuration (e.g., adjusts amount or deliveryMethod), triggering another getGiftCardConfigurationSummary call.
7) Once the API returns isValid = true, the validationError disappears, the summary updates, and Add to Cart becomes enabled.
8) The user then clicks Add to Cart, which calls addConfiguredGiftCardToCart successfully and updates cart-related UI.
interactions: On page load, a controller component triggers getGiftCardOptions to fetch giftCardProducts and supportedDeliveryMethods; while loading, dependent components remain disabled or show a loading state.

When the user selects a different gift card product in the product selector, the selection event updates the current product in state, resets incompatible configuration fields (amount, deliveryMethod) as needed, updates the giftCardProductId query parameter, and triggers getGiftCardConfigurationSummary if enough information is present.
When the user selects a fixed amount option, the amount value in state is updated, the amount query parameter is updated, and getGiftCardConfigurationSummary is called with the current configuration.

When the user enters or edits a custom amount, client-side checks ensure the value is numeric; on blur or after a short debounce, getGiftCardConfigurationSummary is called with the current configuration to enforce server-side rules, and the amount query parameter is updated.

When the user selects a delivery method, the deliveryMethod value in state is updated (restricted to supportedDeliveryMethods), the deliveryMethod query parameter is updated, and getGiftCardConfigurationSummary is called.

When the user types or edits recipientName or message, these values in state are updated, recipientName and message query parameters are updated, and getGiftCardConfigurationSummary is called (immediately or on blur, depending on implementation) to refresh the summary.

When the user changes the quantity (e.g., using an input or stepper), the quantity in state is updated, the quantity query parameter is updated, and getGiftCardConfigurationSummary is called with the new quantity.

Whenever getGiftCardConfigurationSummary resolves, the summary component updates its display with summaryLine and other returned fields; if isValid is false, it shows validationError and signals the Add to Cart component to remain disabled.

When the user clicks the Add to Cart button, the click handler first checks the most recent getGiftCardConfigurationSummary response; if there is no valid response or isValid is false, it either re-requests a summary or shows an error without calling addConfiguredGiftCardToCart.

If the configuration is valid, clicking Add to Cart calls addConfiguredGiftCardToCart with the current giftCardProductId, amount, deliveryMethod, recipientName, message, and quantity; on success, the returned cartItemId, cartItemCount, cartSubtotal, and currency are used to show confirmation feedback and update any cart indicator UI.
If addConfiguredGiftCardToCart returns an error or fails, an error banner component displays a relevant message, and the user can retry after adjusting the configuration.

After a successful Add to Cart, the user can click a View Cart link/button that navigates to cart.html without additional parameters, relying on the server-side cart; alternatively, the user can click a Checkout now link/button that navigates directly to checkout.html.
page_functionality.state_logic:
The page uses URL query parameters to represent and restore the current gift card configuration so that it is shareable and reload-safe, without relying on localStorage or other client-side persistence.

Supported URL query parameters on gift_cards.html include:
- giftCardProductId: ID of the selected GiftCardProduct. On load, the controller checks if this ID exists in the giftCardProducts array returned by getGiftCardOptions and refers to an active product. If valid, it becomes the selected product; if invalid or missing, the controller selects a default product and removes or replaces the invalid parameter in the URL.
- amount: Desired gift card amount. On load, if giftCardProductId is valid, the controller checks whether amount matches one of the product's fixedAmounts or falls within [minCustomAmount, maxCustomAmount] when allowsCustomAmount is true. Only valid values are applied; invalid ones are ignored or cleared from the URL. Amount changes by the user are reflected back into the URL.
- deliveryMethod: Requested delivery method string. On load, this is applied only if it exists within supportedDeliveryMethods returned by getGiftCardOptions. If invalid, the controller either selects a default supported delivery method and updates the URL or leaves it unset until the user chooses.
- recipientName: Pre-filled recipient name. On load, this is used to populate the recipient name field. As the user edits this field, the URL parameter is updated to remain in sync.
- message: Pre-filled message. On load and edit, this is synchronized between the UI state and the message query parameter.
- quantity: Requested quantity. On load, the controller parses this as a positive integer; if parsing fails or the value is not allowed (for example, zero or negative), it falls back to an internal minimum quantity and normalizes or removes the parameter. Quantity changes are reflected back to the URL.

State transitions:
- Initial load: The controller reads current URL parameters, calls getGiftCardOptions, then merges the URL-derived configuration with the server-provided product configuration. After a consistent configuration is constructed (or partially constructed), it calls getGiftCardConfigurationSummary to initialize the summary state.
- Configuration updates: When the user changes any configuration field, the controller updates the in-memory state, synchronizes the relevant query parameters (using history.replaceState or similar to avoid full reloads), and triggers getGiftCardConfigurationSummary. This ensures that the URL always reflects the current configuration while the summary stays in sync.
- Validation errors: If getGiftCardConfigurationSummary returns isValid = false, the current URL remains unchanged (it still describes the attempted configuration), but the UI indicates invalid fields and disables Add to Cart. The user must adjust fields; after changes, the new state and URL are updated, and a new summary is requested.
- After Add to Cart: The successful addConfiguredGiftCardToCart call does not alter the URL parameters for configuration (so the page still reflects the last configured values). Navigation to cart.html or checkout.html occurs via standard links without encoding cart state in parameters, since the cart is maintained server-side.

There is no use of localStorage or other persistent client-side storage; all shareable state is encoded purely via URL parameters and in-memory state managed for the current page session.

components.functionality: On page initialization, this component calls getGiftCardOptions to retrieve the list of available giftCardProducts and supportedDeliveryMethods. It stores these results in page-level state, determines the initial selected gift card product based on the giftCardProductId URL parameter (or a default if none/invalid), and exposes the active product and delivery methods to other components.

Displays all giftCardProducts returned from gift-card-options-loader and allows the user to select one as the active product. When the selection changes, it updates the selected product in shared state, resets dependent configuration fields (amount, deliveryMethod) if they are no longer valid, updates the giftCardProductId query parameter, and triggers recalculation of the configuration summary.

For the currently selected gift card product, this component presents the available amount options. It shows fixedAmounts where applicable and, if allowsCustomAmount is true, provides an input for entering a custom amount within minCustomAmount and maxCustomAmount. On user input, it updates the amount in shared state, updates the amount URL parameter, and requests an updated configuration summary.

Displays the supportedDeliveryMethods returned from getGiftCardOptions (including printable_voucher when available) and allows the user to choose one for the current configuration. It restricts selection to methods contained in supportedDeliveryMethods, updates the deliveryMethod in shared state and the deliveryMethod URL parameter, and triggers an updated configuration summary.

Collects personalization details for the gift card configuration: recipientName and an optional message. It initializes these fields from the recipientName and message URL parameters when present. As the user types or edits, it updates the corresponding values in shared state, synchronizes them to URL parameters, and triggers configuration summary recalculation.

Allows the user to specify how many identical gift cards to purchase with the current configuration. It initializes from the quantity URL parameter when valid, enforces positive integer values, updates the quantity in shared state and the quantity URL parameter, and triggers configuration summary recalculation on change.

Coordinates all configuration inputs (product, amount, deliveryMethod, recipientName, message, quantity) and manages calls to getGiftCardConfigurationSummary. Whenever any part of the configuration changes and is syntactically valid, it calls getGiftCardConfigurationSummary with the current giftCardProductId, amount, deliveryMethod, recipientName, message, and quantity, then stores and distributes the summary (including isValid and validationError) to dependent components such as the summary panel and Add to Cart form.

Displays the current configuration summary returned by getGiftCardConfigurationSummary. Shows summaryLine, amount, currency, deliveryMethod, recipientName, message, quantity when isValid is true. When isValid is false, it prominently displays validationError and indicates which parts of the configuration are invalid where possible. It exposes isValid so that the Add to Cart component can enable or disable its action appropriately.

Handles the Add to Cart action for the configured gift card. The Add to Cart button is enabled only when GiftCardConfigurationSummary indicates isValid = true. On submit, it calls addConfiguredGiftCardToCart with the current giftCardProductId, amount, deliveryMethod, recipientName, message, and quantity. On success, it uses the returned cartItemId, cartItemCount, cartSubtotal, and currency to update cart indicators and success messaging. On failure, it passes error information to the error-banner component.

Provides navigation options after (and optionally before) adding items to the cart. It typically displays a link or button to open cart.html and a Checkout now link or button to navigate directly to checkout.html. It can optionally display cartItemCount and cartSubtotal from the most recent addConfiguredGiftCardToCart response to give the user context before navigating.

Displays error and status messages related to data loading, configuration validation, and adding to cart. It listens for errors from getGiftCardOptions, getGiftCardConfigurationSummary, and addConfiguredGiftCardToCart and shows a concise, actionable message. When the underlying issue is resolved (e.g., after a successful retry), it clears the displayed error.

name: Cart
title: Cart
description: Displays the user’s current cart of gift cards and passes, allows quantity updates and item removal, and provides actions to clear the cart or proceed to checkout.

core_features: Load and display the current cart contents using getCartSummary, including each item’s type, name, detail summary, quantity, unit price, and total price.
Display cart-level subtotal and currency based on getCartSummary; derive and show an estimated total from the same data.
Allow users to update item quantities via updateCartItemQuantity with immediate recalculation of item total and cart subtotal.
Allow users to remove individual items from the cart via removeCartItem and update the cart display and subtotal accordingly.
Allow users to clear all items from the cart via clearCart and show an empty-state view when no items remain.
Provide a primary action to proceed to checkout, navigating to checkout.html with URL parameter orderType=cart.
Provide navigation links to continue shopping on gift_cards.html and passes.html.
Handle URL parameters to control view state (e.g., which item to highlight, which continue-shopping link to emphasize) without affecting backend API calls.
user_workflows:
Review cart contents:
1. User navigates to cart.html (typically via header cart icon).
2. On page load, the controller component calls getCartSummary with no parameters.
3. The response items array and subtotal are bound to the cart items list and summary panel.
4. If hasItems is false or items is empty, the empty-cart component is shown instead of the items list and summary.

Update an item quantity:
1. User clicks a quantity control (e.g., increment, decrement, or edits a numeric field) on a specific cart item row.
2. The cart page controller validates the intended quantity (e.g., ensures it is a positive integer) on the client.
3. The controller calls updateCartItemQuantity with parameters cartItemId and quantity.
4. On success, the response itemTotalPrice and cartSubtotal are applied to the matching cart item row and the summary panel; the cart items list remains visible.
5. If the quantity changes to a value that the backend adjusts (e.g., minimum or maximum enforced), the UI updates to reflect the returned quantity.

Remove an item from the cart:
1. User clicks the remove action on a cart item row.
2. The controller optionally asks for confirmation (client-side) and then calls removeCartItem with the cartItemId.
3. On success, the corresponding item is removed from the items list in the UI.
4. The cart subtotal is updated using the returned cartSubtotal.
5. If remainingItemCount is 0, the controller hides the items list and summary and shows the empty-cart view.

Clear the cart completely:
1. User clicks a clear-all action (e.g., 'Clear cart') in the cart actions area.
2. The controller optionally confirms the action client-side, then calls clearCart with no parameters.
3. On success, the controller clears all items from local state and sets hasItems to false.
4. The page switches to the empty-cart state and hides the checkout action and cart summary.

Proceed to checkout:
1. With at least one item in the cart, user clicks the Checkout button in the summary panel.
2. The controller optionally ensures that the cart view is in sync with the latest getCartSummary data (e.g., by checking that there are items and subtotal > 0 using current in-memory state).
3. The browser navigates to checkout.html with the URL parameter orderType=cart, without additional API calls from this page.
4. The checkout page uses orderType to configure its behavior (outside the scope of this page).

Continue shopping from the cart:
1. From the cart page, user chooses to add more items instead of checking out.
2. User clicks one of the continue-shopping links in the cart actions area.
3. If the user clicks the gift card link, the browser navigates to gift_cards.html with no additional parameters.
4. If the user clicks the passes & packages link, the browser navigates to passes.html with no additional parameters.
5. After adding items on those pages, navigation back to cart.html will reload cart contents via getCartSummary.

Focus on a specific cart item using URL parameters:
1. Another page (e.g., gift_cards.html or passes.html) may redirect to cart.html with a URL parameter such as focusItemId set to a cart item id that was just added.
2. On load, cart.html reads focusItemId from the URL and then calls getCartSummary.
3. After getCartSummary resolves, the controller searches the returned items array for an item whose cartItemId matches focusItemId.
4. If found, the controller instructs the cart items list to scroll to and visually emphasize that item (e.g., via a highlight state); if not found, the cart displays normally.

Adjust cart view behavior using URL parameters:
1. User or upstream pages may include a view parameter in the URL (e.g., cart.html?view=compact or cart.html?view=detailed).
2. On load, cart.html reads the view parameter and stores it in page state only (no API impact).
3. The controller passes this view mode to the cart items list component, which adjusts behaviors such as how much detailSummary text is shown or whether certain columns are displayed.
4. Changing the view parameter (via navigation or internal links) triggers a reload of the page with the new view applied while still using getCartSummary without parameters.
interactions: On page load: cart-page-controller triggers getCartSummary; on success, it populates the cart-items-list and cart-summary-panel; on failure, it shows an error state and a retry option that re-calls getCartSummary.

On quantity increment/decrement click for a cart item: controller calculates the new quantity, validates it, and calls updateCartItemQuantity(cartItemId, quantity); on success, updates item row and cart subtotal; on error, reverts the displayed quantity and shows an inline error.

On direct quantity input change (e.g., typing a number): on blur or submit, controller validates the entered value; if valid, calls updateCartItemQuantity; if invalid, resets the input to the last known valid quantity without calling the API.

On remove item click: controller confirms (if desired) then calls removeCartItem(cartItemId); on success, removes the item from local state and updates subtotal and item count; if remainingItemCount is 0, switches to empty-cart state.

On clear cart click: controller confirms then calls clearCart(); on success, clears local items state, sets hasItems to false, and displays the empty-cart state; on failure, shows an error and leaves existing items visible.

On Checkout button click: if hasItems is true in current state, the browser navigates to checkout.html?orderType=cart; if hasItems is false (e.g., due to a race condition), the controller prevents navigation and prompts the user to refresh cart contents.
On Continue shopping (gift cards) click: browser navigates directly to gift_cards.html with a standard GET request.
On Continue shopping (passes & packages) click: browser navigates directly to passes.html with a standard GET request.
On retry link click in error state: controller re-invokes getCartSummary and attempts to repopulate the cart.

On URL parameter change (e.g., user reloads page with different view or focusItemId): on load, controller re-reads query parameters, calls getCartSummary, and applies highlighting or view mode adjustments accordingly.
page_functionality.state_logic: The cart page does not receive server-side incoming parameters for its core API calls; all interface calls are parameterless except for those operating on a specific cart item. Page state is managed using URL query parameters and in-memory state only. Example URL parameters include: `view` to control layout behavior such as compact vs. detailed cart item display; `focusItemId` to specify a cartItemId that should be highlighted or scrolled into view after getCartSummary returns; and optional navigation-related context such as `from=gift_cards` or `from=passes` that can be used purely on the client to emphasize the relevant continue-shopping link. None of these parameters are forwarded to getCartSummary, updateCartItemQuantity, removeCartItem, or clearCart; those interfaces are always called with their defined parameters only. When the page loads, the controller reads relevant URL parameters first, then calls getCartSummary to obtain the authoritative cart state. All subsequent interactions (quantity updates, removals, clearing the cart) update both the backend (via the provided interfaces) and the in-memory representation of the cart. If the URL parameters change (through navigation or reload), the page reinitializes its state using the new parameters while still using getCartSummary as the single source of truth for cart contents.

components.functionality: Coordinates data loading, URL parameter handling, and communication between cart components. On load, reads URL parameters (view, focusItemId, from) and calls getCartSummary to populate the cart state. Manages in-memory representation of items, subtotal, and hasItems, and handles all calls to updateCartItemQuantity, removeCartItem, and clearCart. Controls navigation to checkout.html and continue-shopping pages.
Fetches the cart summary when invoked and exposes loading, success, and error states. Internally wraps the getCartSummary interface and passes results to the controller.

Displays the collection of cart items returned by getCartSummary, using the view mode from URL parameters. Renders each item as a row via cart-item-row, including itemType, name, detailSummary, quantity, unitPrice, and totalPrice. Supports highlighting a specific item when instructed by the controller (e.g., when focusItemId matches a cartItemId).

Represents a single cart item with controls for quantity changes and item removal. Displays fields from the cart summary: cartItemId, itemType, name, detailSummary, quantity, unitPrice, and totalPrice. Emits events for quantity updates and removal; does not call interfaces directly.

Encapsulates quantity adjustment logic for a cart item. Accepts the current quantity as input and provides user interactions to increase, decrease, or type a new quantity. Validates that the quantity remains a positive integer before emitting a quantity-change event to the controller, which then calls updateCartItemQuantity.

Displays the cart-level subtotal and an estimated total using the currency and subtotal returned by getCartSummary or updated via updateCartItemQuantity and removeCartItem. Shows the total number of items (derived from the items array) and enables the Checkout button when hasItems is true. Emits a proceed-to-checkout event when the Checkout button is clicked.

Provides global cart actions: clearing the cart and continuing shopping. When 'Clear cart' is clicked, emits an event handled by the controller, which then invokes clearCart. Also contains links or buttons for 'Continue shopping' to gift_cards.html and passes.html; these interactions rely on navigation, not API calls. Optionally uses URL parameter `from` to visually emphasize the most relevant continue-shopping link, without changing navigation destinations.

Displays when hasItems is false or when getCartSummary returns an empty items array. Hides the items list and summary panel and shows messaging with continue-shopping actions. Uses the same navigation behavior as cart-actions-bar to send users to gift_cards.html or passes.html.

Shows success, warning, and error messages related to cart operations such as load failures, quantity update errors, or clear-cart failures. Does not interact with any APIs directly, but responds to events and statuses from the controller.
Provides a retry mechanism when getCartSummary fails. When the user clicks retry, it emits an event that instructs the controller to invoke getCartSummary again.

name: Checkout
title: Checkout
description: Page for reviewing order details (cart or single pass), entering buyer information, applying promo codes, choosing a payment method, and completing the purchase with an order confirmation state.

core_features: Detect checkout mode using the URL parameter `orderType` and load either a cart-based summary via `getCartCheckoutSummary` or a single-pass summary via `getPassCheckoutSummary`.
Display a dynamic order summary (items/passes, quantities, subtotal, discounts, and total) based solely on the data returned by the appropriate checkout summary interface.

Provide a promo code input and "Apply" action that re-requests the checkout summary from `getCartCheckoutSummary` or `getPassCheckoutSummary` with the entered promo code, and updates the displayed totals and discount information.
Collect buyer information (name as required, email and phone as optional or as dictated by validation rules) and bind it to the payloads for `completeCartPurchase` or `completePassPurchase`.
Allow the user to choose a payment method (e.g., Pay in Full Now, Pay at Studio, Pay Later) and map the selected option to the `paymentMethod` parameter required by the completion interfaces.

Submit the finalized order to either `completeCartPurchase` or `completePassPurchase` depending on `orderType`, including any applied promo code, and handle loading and error states during submission.
After successful completion, present an order confirmation state using the data returned by the completion interface, including confirmation message, totals, currency, and payment status.
Provide navigation actions from the confirmation state: a link to `my_bookings.html` when the completed purchase includes passes, and a link to `index.html` to return to the home page.
user_workflows:
Workflow 1 – Cart checkout:
1) User navigates from cart.html with URL parameter `orderType=cart`.
2) On page initialization, the page reads `orderType` and calls `getCartCheckoutSummary` with no promo code (or with any internally stored applied promo code if already set) to load the initial summary.
3) The order summary is displayed, listing all items and showing subtotal, any existing discounts, and total.
4) User enters a promo code and clicks the Apply action; the page calls `getCartCheckoutSummary` again with the promo code. If valid, the summary updates with `discountTotal`, `total`, and promo details; if invalid, an error message is shown and the previous totals remain.
5) User fills out buyer information (at minimum, Buyer Name) and optionally enters email and phone.
6) User selects a payment method; the selected method is stored in the checkout state.
7) User clicks the final checkout button. The page sends a request to `completeCartPurchase` with `buyerName`, `buyerEmail`, `buyerPhone`, `paymentMethod`, and the currently applied promo code (if any).
8) While the request is in progress, a submission/loading state is shown and the checkout button is disabled to prevent duplicates.
9) On success, the page stores the returned completion data (orderId, orderType, total, currency, paymentStatus, confirmationMessage) and transitions to the confirmation view.
10) In the confirmation view, the user can optionally click a link to `my_bookings.html` if the returned `orderType` indicates passes are included, or click a link to `index.html` to return home.

Workflow 2 – Direct pass checkout:
1) User navigates from pass_detail.html with URL parameters `orderType=pass`, `passProductId`, `quantity`, and `startDate`.
2) On page initialization, the page validates that all required parameters for pass checkout are present; if so, it calls `getPassCheckoutSummary` with `passProductId`, `quantity`, `startDate`, and no promo code (or with any internally stored applied promo code if already set).
3) The pass checkout summary is displayed, including pass name, quantity, start date, calculated expiration (if present in response), unit price, subtotal, any discounts, and total.
4) User enters a promo code and clicks Apply. The page calls `getPassCheckoutSummary` again with the same `passProductId`, `quantity`, `startDate`, and the entered promo code, then updates the summary with any discount and promo description from the response.
5) User completes the buyer information form (Buyer Name required; Buyer Email at minimum if needed for pass communication, depending on validation rules defined for the page).
6) User selects a payment method and the choice is stored in the checkout state.
7) User clicks the final checkout button. The page sends a request to `completePassPurchase` with `passProductId`, `quantity`, `startDate`, `buyerName`, `buyerEmail`, `paymentMethod`, and the applied promo code (if any).
8) A loading state is shown while the completion call is in progress and the button is disabled.
9) On success, the page stores the returned completion data (`orderId`, `passPurchaseId`, `orderTotal`, `currency`, `paymentStatus`, `confirmationMessage`) and transitions to the confirmation view.
10) In the confirmation view, the user sees the confirmation message and totals, and can click a link to `my_bookings.html` (to view passes and bookings) or click a link to `index.html` to return home.

Workflow 3 – Promo code editing during checkout:
1) User has already applied a promo code and sees discounted totals in the summary.
2) User modifies the promo code text and clicks Apply again.
3) The page re-calls the appropriate summary interface (`getCartCheckoutSummary` or `getPassCheckoutSummary`) using the updated code and replaces the previously applied promo state with the new response.
4) If the new code is invalid or returns no discount, the page displays an error and uses the monetary values returned by the latest response (which may reflect no discount). The stored "applied promo code" in the checkout state is updated to match the code actually accepted by the backend (or cleared if none).
5) When the user completes the order, the promo code sent to `completeCartPurchase` or `completePassPurchase` is exactly the one stored as applied in the checkout state, ensuring consistency between summary and completion.
interactions: On page load, read `orderType` (and when `orderType='pass'`, also `passProductId`, `quantity`, and `startDate`) from the URL. Based on `orderType`, call either `getCartCheckoutSummary` or `getPassCheckoutSummary` to populate the initial summary state.
When the user types into the promo code field, update the in-memory promo input value but do not affect the totals until the user triggers the Apply action.

When the user clicks the promo Apply button (or submits the promo form), call the appropriate summary interface with the current promo code input and update the summary display and applied promo state from the response. Handle and display any errors returned or inferred (e.g., no discount applied).

As the user types into buyer information fields, update the buyer state object (buyerName, buyerEmail, buyerPhone) and run client-side validation (e.g., required fields, basic email pattern) before enabling the final checkout button.
When the user selects a payment method option, update the payment method in the checkout state to the corresponding `paymentMethod` value required by the completion interface.

When the user clicks the final checkout button, validate that required fields are present (e.g., buyerName, paymentMethod, and any other rules), then call `completeCartPurchase` or `completePassPurchase` with the current buyer details, payment method, and applied promo code. Disable the button and show a loading indication during the call.

On successful completion, transition the page from the editable checkout view to a read-only confirmation view, displaying confirmation details from the completion response and hiding or locking the input fields.

In the confirmation view, when the user clicks the "View my passes & bookings" link, navigate to `my_bookings.html` (only shown when the completed purchase includes passes, determined from completion response data).
In the confirmation view, when the user clicks the "Return to Home" link, navigate to `index.html`.
page_functionality.state_logic: The primary external state comes from URL parameters. The page always reads `orderType` from the URL. When `orderType='cart'`, the cart checkout path is used and the page calls `getCartCheckoutSummary`. When `orderType='pass'`, the page additionally expects `passProductId`, `quantity`, and `startDate` in the URL; if any of these are missing or invalid, the page enters an error state instead of calling `getPassCheckoutSummary`. The promo code is not read from the URL and exists only in in-memory state: there is a 'promoInput' value (what the user has typed) and an 'appliedPromoCode' value (what was most recently accepted by a summary interface response). Whenever the promo Apply interaction occurs, the page calls the correct summary interface with the current 'promoInput', and the resulting `appliedPromoCode`, `promoDescription`, `discountTotal`, and `total` fields from the response are used to update the summary and the 'appliedPromoCode' in state. The current buyer information (buyerName, buyerEmail, buyerPhone) and selected payment method are also stored in in-memory state, updated on every input change. A 'mode' or 'viewState' flag is maintained internally with values such as 'loadingSummary', 'readyForInput', 'submittingOrder', 'confirmation', and 'error'; this flag determines which components are active at any given time. Upon successful completion via `completeCartPurchase` or `completePassPurchase`, the completion response is stored as 'completionData' and the viewState transitions to 'confirmation'; from this point, further calls to summary interfaces are not made, and navigation links to `my_bookings.html` and `index.html` become available. The presence or absence of the "View my passes & bookings" link in the confirmation state is determined by the completion data (e.g., orderType for cart purchases or the existence of a passPurchaseId for pass purchases), without any additional URL parameters.

components.functionality: Coordinates the overall checkout flow: reads URL parameters, determines whether the user is in cart or pass checkout mode, triggers the appropriate summary interface call, manages in-memory state for buyer details, promo code, payment method, loading/submitting flags, and routes between the edit view and the confirmation view.
data_binding: URL params (when orderType='pass'): passProductId, quantity, startDate
event_handlers: onInit: Read `orderType` (and `passProductId`, `quantity`, `startDate` if needed) from the URL and call the corresponding summary interface with no promo code to initialize the summary state.

onPromoApplied(summaryResponse): Update the centralized checkout state with fields from the summary response (items or pass details, subtotal, discountTotal, total, currency, appliedPromoCode, promoDescription) and set viewState to 'readyForInput'.
onBuyerInfoChange(updatedBuyerState): Merge the updated buyer details into the central checkout state and re-evaluate validation status.
onPaymentMethodChange(selectedMethod): Store the selected payment method in the central checkout state.
onSubmitOrderStart(): Set viewState to 'submittingOrder' to drive loading indicators and disable inputs.

onSubmitOrderSuccess(completionResponse): Store the completion data (orderId, orderType or passPurchaseId, totals, paymentStatus, confirmationMessage) in state and transition viewState to 'confirmation'.
onSubmitOrderError(error): Store error information and set viewState to an error state suitable for displaying a failure message and re-enabling submission.
onNavigateToBookings(): Trigger navigation to my_bookings.html when available in the confirmation state.
onNavigateHome(): Trigger navigation to index.html from the confirmation state.

components.functionality: Shows the current order summary based on the latest summary interface response. In cart mode, this includes a list of items with names, detail summaries, quantities, and per-item totals. In pass mode, it shows pass name, quantity, start date, expiration date (if provided), unit price, subtotal, discount total, and final total. Also displays currency and any promo description.
data_binding: Interface response (cart mode): getCartCheckoutSummary.subtotal, getCartCheckoutSummary.discountTotal, getCartCheckoutSummary.total, getCartCheckoutSummary.currency, getCartCheckoutSummary.appliedPromoCode, getCartCheckoutSummary.promoDescription

Interface response (pass mode): getPassCheckoutSummary.passName, getPassCheckoutSummary.quantity, getPassCheckoutSummary.startDate, getPassCheckoutSummary.expirationDate, getPassCheckoutSummary.unitPrice, getPassCheckoutSummary.subtotal, getPassCheckoutSummary.discountTotal, getPassCheckoutSummary.total, getPassCheckoutSummary.currency, getPassCheckoutSummary.appliedPromoCode, getPassCheckoutSummary.promoDescription
event_handlers: onSummaryUpdated(summaryData): Re-render the list of items or pass details and the monetary amounts whenever the state manager updates the summary data.
onViewStateChange(newViewState): Hide or make read-only when the viewState becomes 'confirmation' so that the summary appears as read-only review information after purchase.

components.functionality: Allows the user to input a promo code and request that it be applied to the current checkout. When submitted, it triggers a new summary calculation via the correct summary interface and updates the summary and applied promo state.
data_binding: Central checkout state: appliedPromoCode (the code last accepted by the backend)
Interface: getCartCheckoutSummary (for cart mode, with parameter promoCode)
Interface: getPassCheckoutSummary (for pass mode, with parameters passProductId, quantity, startDate, promoCode)
event_handlers: onInputChange(value): Update the promoInput state as the user types.

onSubmit(event): Prevent default form submission, determine current checkout mode from the state manager, and call either getCartCheckoutSummary({ promoCode: promoInput }) or getPassCheckoutSummary({ passProductId, quantity, startDate, promoCode: promoInput }).
onApplySuccess(summaryResponse): Notify the state manager with the new summary data so that appliedPromoCode and pricing details are updated.
onApplyError(error): Display an inline error message and keep the previously applied promo and totals as defined by the last successful summary response.

components.functionality: Collects and validates buyer information required for the order, including buyer name (required), and email/phone as configured. Keeps this data synchronized with the central checkout state for later use in order completion calls.
event_handlers: onBuyerNameChange(value): Update buyerName in the central checkout state and run required-field validation.
onBuyerEmailChange(value): Update buyerEmail in the central checkout state and optionally run pattern validation.
onBuyerPhoneChange(value): Update buyerPhone in the central checkout state and optionally run formatting validation.
onBlur(field): Mark the field as 'touched' for displaying validation messages.
onViewStateChange(newViewState): Disable or make fields read-only when newViewState is 'submittingOrder' or 'confirmation'.

components.functionality: Presents available payment options to the user and maps the UI choices to the backend `paymentMethod` values required by the completion interfaces.
event_handlers: onSelect(optionId): Translate the selected option into one of the allowed values ('pay_in_full_now', 'pay_at_studio', or 'pay_later') and update the central checkout state.
onViewStateChange(newViewState): Disable changes to the selected payment method when the viewState is 'submittingOrder' or 'confirmation'.

components.functionality: Combines buyer info, payment method, and current summary into a final review section with a single primary action to complete the purchase. Performs client-side validation and triggers the appropriate completion interface based on the checkout mode.
data_binding: Central checkout state: buyerName, buyerEmail, buyerPhone
Central checkout state: summaryData (cart or pass mode)
event_handlers:
onClickSubmit():
- Run validation to ensure required buyer fields and a payment method are set.
- If validation passes, call onSubmitOrderStart on the state manager and then, depending on `orderType`, call either completeCartPurchase({ buyerName, buyerEmail, buyerPhone, paymentMethod, promoCode: appliedPromoCode }) or completePassPurchase({ passProductId, quantity, startDate, buyerName, buyerEmail, paymentMethod, promoCode: appliedPromoCode }).
onCompletionSuccess(completionResponse): Pass the response to the state manager's onSubmitOrderSuccess handler.
onCompletionError(error): Pass the error to the state manager's onSubmitOrderError handler and re-enable submission.
onViewStateChange(newViewState): Disable the submit button and show a loading state when newViewState is 'submittingOrder'; hide the entire control when newViewState is 'confirmation'.

components.functionality: Displays confirmation details after a successful purchase, using data returned from the completion interface. Shows confirmation message, total amount, currency, payment status, and offers navigation links to view bookings/passes (when relevant) or return home.
data_binding: Completion response (cart mode): completeCartPurchase.orderId, completeCartPurchase.orderType, completeCartPurchase.total, completeCartPurchase.currency, completeCartPurchase.paymentStatus, completeCartPurchase.confirmationMessage

Completion response (pass mode): completePassPurchase.orderId, completePassPurchase.passPurchaseId, completePassPurchase.orderTotal, completePassPurchase.currency, completePassPurchase.paymentStatus, completePassPurchase.confirmationMessage
event_handlers: onViewStateChange(newViewState): Render this panel only when newViewState is 'confirmation'.

onClickViewBookings(): If the completion response indicates a pass purchase (e.g., presence of passPurchaseId in pass mode or appropriate orderType in cart mode), call the state manager's onNavigateToBookings to navigate to my_bookings.html.
onClickReturnHome(): Call the state manager's onNavigateHome to navigate to index.html.

components.functionality: Shows high-level status messages such as loading, submission in progress, errors fetching the summary, or errors completing the order. Provides consistent feedback across all checkout states.
event_handlers: onViewStateChange(newViewState): Update the displayed status text (e.g., show loading when fetching summary or submitting order) based on the new viewState.
onErrorChanged(errorDetails): When error details are updated by the state manager, display or hide an error message accordingly.

name: Wishlist
title: Wishlist
description: Shows the user’s saved classes and workshops, with sorting and weekday overview, and links to class details for booking.

core_features: Check authentication status before loading wishlist content using getAuthStatus.
Retrieve all wishlist items for the authenticated user, with optional sorting, using getWishlistItems(sortBy).
Display wishlist items in a structured list including title, priceDisplay, primaryWeekday, categoryName, audience, and ageRangeLabel.
Allow users to change the wishlist sort order (e.g., by weekday, price, category) by updating the sortBy URL parameter and re-calling getWishlistItems.
Group or visually cluster wishlist items by primaryWeekday when sortBy=weekday to make weekday coverage easy to verify.
Provide a weekday summary derived from wishlist items’ primaryWeekday values so users can check that their saved classes span different weekdays.
Enable navigation from each wishlist item to its corresponding class_detail.html page using the classOfferingId from getWishlistItems.
Allow users to remove individual items from the wishlist by calling removeWishlistItem(wishlistItemId) and updating the list based on the response.
Handle an empty wishlist state by displaying guidance and a navigation link to classes.html?categoryId=kids_teens via the specified outgoing connection.
Keep wishlist sort state in the URL via the sortBy parameter so that back/forward navigation and sharing the URL preserves the chosen sort order.
user_workflows:
Workflow: Initial page load and authentication
1. User opens wishlist.html, optionally with a sortBy URL parameter.
2. The page controller reads sortBy from the URL and validates it.
3. The page calls getAuthStatus().
4. If getAuthStatus().isAuthenticated is false, the page shows a sign-in prompt section and does not call getWishlistItems yet.
5. If isAuthenticated is true, the page calls getWishlistItems(sortBy) with the current sortBy value (or a default if none is provided).
6. The page renders the wishlist list, weekday summary, and sorting controls based on the returned data.

Workflow: Changing sort order
1. User interacts with the sort controls (e.g., selects a different sort option such as weekday, price_low_high, or category).
2. The sort control updates the sortBy value in the URL query string without changing other parameters.
3. The page controller detects the sortBy change (via router or URL change listener).
4. The page calls getWishlistItems(newSortBy) with the updated value.
5. The wishlist list re-renders in the new order; if sortBy=weekday, items are also grouped by primaryWeekday.
6. The weekday summary component recomputes its counts based on the newly fetched data (which may already be sorted server-side).

Workflow: Verifying weekday coverage for saved classes (e.g., for kids’ classes)
1. User ensures they are authenticated and viewing their wishlist.
2. User selects the weekday sort option in the sort controls so that sortBy=weekday is applied to the URL and getWishlistItems is re-called.
3. The weekday summary component derives, from the getWishlistItems response, how many wishlist items are associated with each primaryWeekday.
4. The user reviews the weekday summary to confirm that their saved classes span different weekdays.
5. The user can scroll through grouped wishlist items (clustered by primaryWeekday) to inspect details such as audience and ageRangeLabel for each saved class.

Workflow: Removing an item from the wishlist
1. User views their wishlist list with items loaded via getWishlistItems.
2. For a specific wishlist item, user clicks the remove action (e.g., a button or icon) bound to that item’s wishlistItemId.
3. The page calls removeWishlistItem(wishlistItemId) for the selected item.
4. If removeWishlistItem returns success=true, the page removes that item from the in-memory list and updates the display without a full page reload.
5. The weekday summary is recalculated based on the updated list.
6. If totalWishlistItems from the response indicates that there are no remaining items, the list view is replaced with the empty state component.
7. If removeWishlistItem returns success=false, the page displays an inline error message associated with that item and leaves the item visible.

Workflow: Navigating from wishlist to class details for booking
1. User views their wishlist list with items loaded via getWishlistItems.
2. User clicks on the title or main clickable region of a wishlist item card.
3. The page navigates to class_detail.html, passing the item’s classOfferingId as the id URL parameter (class_detail.html?id=<classOfferingId>).
4. The class detail page (outside of this spec) then handles showing schedule and booking options.
5. After reviewing details or booking, the user can use the browser back button to return to wishlist.html, which re-reads the sortBy parameter and re-calls getWishlistItems to restore the wishlist view.

Workflow: Using the empty state to find more classes
1. User navigates to wishlist.html while authenticated but has no items in their wishlist.
2. getWishlistItems(sortBy) returns an empty array.
3. The page displays the empty state component instead of the list.
4. User clicks the provided helper link to browse more classes targeted for younger audiences.
5. The page navigates to classes.html?categoryId=kids_teens using the defined outgoing connection.
6. From the classes listing page, users may add new classes to their wishlist (handled outside this spec), which will later appear when returning to wishlist.html.
interactions: Page load: Triggers getAuthStatus(), then conditionally getWishlistItems(sortBy) if the user is authenticated.
URL sortBy parameter change: When sortBy in the URL changes, the page controller calls getWishlistItems(newSortBy) and re-renders all dependent components.
Sort option selection: User selects a sort option in the sort controls; event handler updates the sortBy URL parameter and does not directly manipulate the list data.
Wishlist item title/card click: Navigates to class_detail.html with id set to the clicked item’s classOfferingId.
Remove wishlist item click: Calls removeWishlistItem(wishlistItemId); on success, removes the item from the local list and refreshes dependent views (weekday summary, empty-state check).

Authentication state change: If a global auth change occurs (e.g., user signs in elsewhere and returns), the page controller can re-invoke getAuthStatus() and, once authenticated, call getWishlistItems(currentSortBy).
Empty state link click: Navigates to classes.html?categoryId=kids_teens via the specified outgoing connection.

Error handling interaction: If getWishlistItems or removeWishlistItem fail, the page shows non-blocking, inline error messages and provides a retry interaction (e.g., a retry button that invokes the same interface again).
page_functionality.state_logic: The primary page state is driven by the sortBy URL query parameter and the results of the assigned interfaces. On initial load, the page reads window.location.search to obtain sortBy. If sortBy is missing or not recognized, the page applies a default sort mode defined in the client and calls getWishlistItems with that default value. Valid values for sortBy are constrained to those supported by the backend (including but not limited to 'weekday', 'price_low_high', and 'category'). The chosen sortBy value is always passed directly into getWishlistItems(sortBy) without being transformed into any static or hardcoded data. When the user changes the sort selection in the UI, the event handler updates the sortBy parameter in the URL (e.g., by pushing a new history state) and triggers a refresh of the wishlist data by calling getWishlistItems with the new sortBy value. The current wishlist contents are kept in memory only as long as the page is active and are not persisted in localStorage or other client storage; reloading the page always re-runs getAuthStatus and getWishlistItems based on the URL. Authentication state is derived exclusively from getAuthStatus(). If isAuthenticated is false, the page does not call getWishlistItems and instead maintains a 'requiresAuth' view state that shows only sign-in guidance. If isAuthenticated is true, the page transitions to a 'wishlistLoaded' or 'wishlistEmpty' state depending on the array length returned by getWishlistItems. The weekday summary component and grouping behavior derive their state purely from the in-memory representation of the getWishlistItems response (using primaryWeekday) and recompute whenever that response changes (due to a new sortBy or item removal). Removal of an item calls removeWishlistItem(wishlistItemId) and, on success, updates the in-memory list and all derived views; the totalWishlistItems from the response is used as a consistency check and to determine when to transition to the 'wishlistEmpty' state, but no static counts are assumed. Back/forward navigation is handled naturally because the sortBy parameter in the URL is the single source of truth for sorting-related state, and the page always re-reads this parameter before calling getWishlistItems.

components.functionality: Coordinates initial loading and core state transitions: reads the sortBy URL parameter, checks authentication via getAuthStatus, invokes getWishlistItems when appropriate, stores the current wishlist dataset in memory, and notifies child components (auth gate, sort controls, weekday summary, list, empty state) when data or auth state changes.

Shows or hides wishlist content based on authentication status. When isAuthenticated=false, it displays a message guiding the user to sign in and suppresses any wishlist list rendering. When isAuthenticated=true, it reveals the sort controls, weekday summary, and wishlist list as provided by the page controller.

Allows the user to select the sorting preference for their wishlist. It reads the current sortBy state from the page controller, displays the available sort options consistent with backend support, and when a user selects a different option, it requests the page controller to update the URL sortBy parameter and re-fetch data via getWishlistItems.

Derives a summary of wishlist items by primaryWeekday to help users quickly assess which weekdays they have saved classes for. It listens to changes in the wishlist dataset (returned from getWishlistItems) and computes counts and presence for each weekday value without introducing any static or hardcoded class information.

Renders the list of wishlist items returned by getWishlistItems. When sortBy=weekday, it organizes items into logical weekday groupings based on primaryWeekday. Each item row/card includes key fields such as title, priceDisplay, primaryWeekday, categoryName, audience, and ageRangeLabel, and exposes actions for navigating to class_detail.html and removing the item from the wishlist.

Represents a single wishlist item within the list. It receives one object from the getWishlistItems result (containing wishlistItemId, classOfferingId, title, categoryName, priceDisplay, isFree, primaryWeekday, audience, ageRangeLabel) and binds these to functional UI elements. The main clickable area navigates to class_detail.html?id=classOfferingId, and a dedicated remove control triggers removeWishlistItem for that wishlistItemId.

Encapsulates the logic for removing items from the wishlist. When invoked with a wishlistItemId, it calls removeWishlistItem(wishlistItemId). If success=true, it notifies the page controller to remove the corresponding item from the in-memory wishlist dataset and to recompute derived state (weekday summary, empty-list condition). If success=false or the call fails, it triggers an error state that can be surfaced by the wishlist-item-card component.

Displays when getWishlistItems returns an empty array for an authenticated user. It hides the list and weekday summary and shows explanatory text plus a helper link that navigates to classes.html with categoryId set to kids_teens, using the specified outgoing connection.

name: My Bookings
title: My Bookings
description: Page where authenticated users can review, filter, and sort their upcoming and past art class bookings, quickly access bookings occurring soon, and navigate to booking details or new class bookings.

core_features: Check authentication status and gate access to bookings, showing a login form when the user is not authenticated.
Fetch a summary of the user's bookings (upcoming and past) based on the current status and sort preferences using getMyBookingsSummary.
Display a list of upcoming bookings including title, type, date/time range, location summary, booking status, and rescheduling eligibility indicators.
Visually emphasize upcoming bookings that are within a short time window using the isWithin7Days flag from getMyBookingsSummary.
Display a list of past bookings for reference, with quick access to detailed information on each booking.
Provide controls to filter bookings by status (such as upcoming, past, or all) and to sort bookings by date via the statusFilter and sortBy URL parameters.
Allow navigation from any booking entry to the Booking Detail page using the bookingId parameter.
Provide a shortcut to browse and book additional classes by navigating to the classes listing with the appropriate categoryId parameter.
Serve as a stable hub page that can be reloaded (with preserved statusFilter and sortBy URL parameters) after users reschedule or update bookings on other pages.
user_workflows:
Initial page load (authenticated user):
1. The page initializes and calls getAuthStatus.
2. If isAuthenticated is true, the page reads statusFilter and sortBy from the current URL query string.
3. If any of these parameters are missing or invalid, the page derives appropriate default values and updates the URL to include them without reloading the entire document.
4. The page calls getMyBookingsSummary with statusFilter and sortBy from the URL.
5. When getMyBookingsSummary resolves, the page populates the upcoming and past bookings lists, updates counts and highlighting based on the returned data, and shows any bookings that are within the short-interval window via the isWithin7Days flag.

Initial page load (unauthenticated user):
1. The page initializes and calls getAuthStatus.
2. If isAuthenticated is false, the page hides bookings-related components and shows the login form component.
3. The user enters credentials and submits the form, which calls loginWithCredentials.
4. If loginWithCredentials returns isAuthenticated as true, the page hides the login form, shows bookings components, reads/initializes statusFilter and sortBy from the URL, and calls getMyBookingsSummary.
5. If authentication fails, the login form displays the message returned from loginWithCredentials without calling getMyBookingsSummary.

Filtering bookings by status:
1. The user interacts with the status filter control (for example, selecting upcoming, past, or all), which is managed by the filter bar component.
2. The filter bar emits a filter change event with the chosen statusFilter value.
3. The state controller updates the URL's statusFilter parameter to the new value, using history APIs so the browser back/forward buttons remain consistent.
4. The state controller calls getMyBookingsSummary with the updated statusFilter and the current sortBy from the URL.
5. The upcoming and past booking lists are re-rendered based on the new data, and any counts, empty states, and highlighting adjust automatically.

Sorting bookings by date:
1. The user selects a different sort order from a sort control (for example, ascending or descending by date).
2. The filter bar emits a sort change event with the new sortBy value.
3. The state controller updates the URL's sortBy parameter.
4. The state controller calls getMyBookingsSummary with the current statusFilter and updated sortBy.
5. The booking lists re-render in the new order as provided by getMyBookingsSummary.

Identifying bookings occurring soon and rescheduling eligibility:
1. After getMyBookingsSummary returns, the upcoming bookings list iterates through each upcoming booking.
2. For each booking where isWithin7Days is true, the list applies a distinct visual emphasis and may surface additional contextual text indicating that this booking is occurring soon.
3. For each booking where canReschedule is true, the list may render a rescheduling indicator and ensure that the row is clearly interactive.
4. The user clicks on a reschedulable booking row.
5. The page navigates to booking_detail.html with bookingId set to that booking's id, where rescheduling can be managed.
6. After rescheduling on the booking detail page, if the user returns to my_bookings.html (e.g., via browser navigation or a link), the current statusFilter and sortBy URL parameters are read again and getMyBookingsSummary is called, reflecting any changes.

Viewing booking details from My Bookings:
1. The user scans the upcoming list and selects a booking of interest.
2. The user clicks on the booking row (or an explicit detail action within the row).
3. The page constructs the URL booking_detail.html?bookingId=<selectedBookingId> and initiates navigation.
4. When the user later returns to my_bookings.html, the page re-reads statusFilter and sortBy from the URL and reloads data via getMyBookingsSummary to act as a central hub.

Accessing past bookings for reference:
1. The user selects the past or all status in the filter bar, if not already selected.
2. The state controller updates the statusFilter URL parameter and calls getMyBookingsSummary.
3. The past bookings list renders the items from the past array, including the basic snapshot information (title, booking type, startDateTime, bookingStatus).
4. The user may click on any past booking entry to navigate to booking_detail.html with the appropriate bookingId parameter for additional details or receipts, if available.

Booking another class from My Bookings:
1. The user scrolls to the end of the upcoming bookings list or an empty state message indicating there are no upcoming bookings.
2. The user clicks a control that indicates booking another class.
3. The page navigates to classes.html with categoryId set to the value used for the full classes listing.
4. After creating a new booking on subsequent pages, the user can navigate back to my_bookings.html, which will re-load bookings based on the current or default URL parameters.

Using browser navigation with preserved state:
1. The user changes statusFilter and sortBy multiple times while exploring bookings.
2. Each change updates the URL parameters through the state controller.
3. When the user uses the browser back and forward buttons, the popstate event is captured by the state controller.
4. For each popstate, the state controller reads statusFilter and sortBy from the URL and calls getMyBookingsSummary again.
5. The booking lists update so that the view always matches the URL, ensuring a consistent and restorable page state.
interactions: Page initialization: triggers getAuthStatus and, upon successful authentication, getMyBookingsSummary with parameters derived from the URL.
Login form submission: calls loginWithCredentials using the entered username and password, then conditionally shows bookings content and triggers getMyBookingsSummary if authentication succeeds.
Status filter selection: changes statusFilter, updates the URL query parameter, and calls getMyBookingsSummary with the new filter and current sortBy.
Sort selection: changes sortBy, updates the corresponding URL query parameter, and calls getMyBookingsSummary with the updated sort value and current statusFilter.
Upcoming booking row click: reads the associated bookingId and navigates to booking_detail.html?bookingId=<bookingId>.
Past booking row click: reads the associated bookingId and similarly navigates to booking_detail.html with that bookingId.
Book-another-class button click: navigates to classes.html with categoryId set to the appropriate listing category value.
Browser back/forward button usage: triggers a handler that reads the latest statusFilter and sortBy from the URL and re-calls getMyBookingsSummary to keep content synchronized.
Error handling: when any interface call fails, a dedicated error component displays the error, and allows the user to retry the operation.

Empty state handling: when the upcoming or past arrays returned by getMyBookingsSummary are empty (subject to the current filter), the corresponding list components show contextually appropriate empty-state messaging and controls, such as the option to navigate to classes.html.
page_functionality.state_logic: The page state is fully managed through URL parameters combined with interface responses, without using localStorage. On initial load, the page reads statusFilter and sortBy from the query string (for example, my_bookings.html?statusFilter=<value>&sortBy=<value>). If parameters are missing or invalid, the state controller determines defaults that are compatible with the getMyBookingsSummary interface and then updates the URL query string using history APIs without a full page reload. These parameters are then passed directly into getMyBookingsSummary so the server returns appropriately filtered and sorted data. Whenever the user changes filters or sorting, the state controller updates the corresponding URL parameters first, and only then calls getMyBookingsSummary with the new values, ensuring that the visual state always corresponds to the URL. The browser back and forward buttons are wired to re-read these parameters from the URL and to re-fetch bookings by invoking getMyBookingsSummary with the restored statusFilter and sortBy values. Authentication status is not stored in URL parameters; instead, each visit or reload starts by calling getAuthStatus. If authentication fails, the bookings view is suppressed and the login form is shown. On successful login (via loginWithCredentials), the page does not alter URL parameters other than those related to filters and sorting; it simply calls getMyBookingsSummary using the current URL-derived state, so the user returns to the same filter/sort context they had selected before or the default context if none had been set.

components.functionality: Controls access to the My Bookings content based on authentication status. On initialization it calls getAuthStatus; if the user is authenticated, it reveals the bookings area and instructs the bookings state controller to load data. If not authenticated, it hides bookings-related components and displays the login form component.

Provides fields for username and password, and handles user authentication. On submit, it calls loginWithCredentials. If authentication succeeds, it notifies the auth-status-gate to switch the view to bookings mode and triggers an initial bookings load. If it fails, it displays the message returned by loginWithCredentials.

Centralizes state management for My Bookings. Reads and normalizes statusFilter and sortBy from the URL, updates these parameters when filters change, and calls getMyBookingsSummary with the current state. It also listens for browser navigation events and re-fetches bookings to keep the view synchronized with the URL.

Renders controls for filtering and sorting bookings, such as a status toggle (upcoming/past/all) and a sort selector for date ordering. It does not call interfaces directly; instead, it emits events with the chosen statusFilter and sortBy values that the bookings-state-controller uses to update the URL and call getMyBookingsSummary.

Displays high-level information derived from the upcoming and past arrays returned by getMyBookingsSummary, such as total counts of upcoming and past bookings, and a count of upcoming bookings that are within the short lead time window. It updates whenever getMyBookingsSummary data changes.

Renders the list of upcoming bookings from getMyBookingsSummary. For each booking, it shows key details including classTitle, bookingType, startDateTime, endDateTime, locationSummary, bookingStatus, daysUntilStart, and any indicators based on isWithin7Days and canReschedule. It highlights bookings where isWithin7Days is true, indicates whether rescheduling is allowed based on canReschedule, and supports navigation to booking_detail.html by clicking on the booking entry. If the upcoming array is empty under the current filter, it shows an appropriate empty state and a control to book another class.

Renders the list of past bookings from getMyBookingsSummary. For each booking, it displays classTitle, bookingType, startDateTime, and bookingStatus. It optionally allows navigation to booking_detail.html when a past booking entry is selected, using the bookingId. When the past array is empty under the current filter, it shows an appropriate empty state message.

Provides a dedicated control that navigates the user from My Bookings to the main classes listing page when they want to book additional classes or workshops. It constructs a URL to classes.html with the appropriate categoryId parameter value representing the complete classes listing.

Displays error messages when calls to getAuthStatus, loginWithCredentials, or getMyBookingsSummary fail. It provides retry actions that re-invoke the failed interface call via the bookings-state-controller or auth-status-gate.

Shows and hides a loading state while interface calls are in progress. It is activated when getMyBookingsSummary, getAuthStatus, or loginWithCredentials are pending, and deactivated when they resolve or fail.

name: Booking Detail
title: Booking Detail
description: Shows details for a specific booking and, when allowed, lets the user reschedule it to a future session that meets policy rules.

core_features: Read bookingId from the URL query string and load booking details via getBookingDetail.
Check authentication state via getAuthStatus and prevent booking data from loading for unauthenticated users.
Display booking information including class title, type, start/end date and time, location, participants, booking status, payment method, payment status, and reschedule policy summary.
Show how many days remain until the booking start and precomputed flags for timing windows (e.g., isWithin7Days, isAtLeast14DaysAway).
Conditionally render a reschedule call-to-action when canReschedule is true and bookingStatus is appropriate (as provided by getBookingDetail).
When the user starts rescheduling, load reschedule options via getRescheduleOptions, including earliestAllowedNewDate and suggestedSessions.
Provide an interface to select a new session (newClassSessionId) from suggestedSessions, highlighting which options match the original time slot (isSameTimeSlot) and which have available spots.
Validate the user’s new session selection against earliestAllowedNewDate and session capacity information before enabling confirmation.
Submit the reschedule request via rescheduleBooking with bookingId and newClassSessionId and handle success/error responses, updating the visible booking details after success.
Present clear feedback messages for all key actions (loading, errors, ineligible reschedule attempts, successful rescheduling) using only dynamic values from API responses where applicable.
Offer navigation back to the My Bookings page via a breadcrumb/link that routes to my_bookings.html.
user_workflows: View booking details: 1) User navigates to booking_detail.html with a bookingId query parameter. 2) Page checks authentication via getAuthStatus. 3) If authenticated and bookingId is present, page calls getBookingDetail(bookingId). 4) Page displays booking information including schedule, location, participants, status, payment info, reschedule policy summary, and days-until-start information. 5) User may navigate back to My Bookings via a link to my_bookings.html.

Attempt to reschedule an eligible booking: 1) On the booking detail view, user sees that rescheduling is allowed based on canReschedule from getBookingDetail. 2) User clicks the reschedule action. 3) Page updates the URL (e.g., adds an action query parameter representing reschedule mode) and calls getRescheduleOptions(bookingId). 4) Page shows earliestAllowedNewDate and a list or calendar of suggestedSessions. 5) User selects one of the available sessions that satisfies the policy (implicitly enforced by suggestedSessions and earliestAllowedNewDate). 6) User clicks a confirmation action to proceed. 7) Page calls rescheduleBooking(bookingId, newClassSessionId). 8) On success, page displays a confirmation message and refreshes booking details via getBookingDetail(bookingId) so the updated schedule is shown. 9) User may return to My Bookings or stay on the page.

Reschedule validation failure: 1) User is in reschedule mode with suggestedSessions rendered. 2) User attempts to confirm without selecting a session; the page blocks submission and shows a validation message. 3) If submission is made with a session that fails back-end validation (as indicated by rescheduleBooking response), the page shows the returned message and keeps the reschedule interface open. 4) User can choose a different suggestedSession and try again.

Handling ineligible or late reschedule attempts: 1) User opens a booking where canReschedule is false or isWithin7Days is true. 2) Page hides or disables the reschedule action and surfaces the reschedulePolicySummary explaining the reason, using only the text returned by getBookingDetail. 3) User can still review booking details and navigate back to My Bookings.

Page refresh or direct deep-link to reschedule mode: 1) User or another page links to booking_detail.html with bookingId and an action query parameter representing reschedule mode. 2) After loading booking details, page detects the action parameter and, if canReschedule is true, automatically opens the reschedule interface and calls getRescheduleOptions(bookingId). 3) If canReschedule is false, the page ignores reschedule mode and shows a message derived from reschedulePolicySummary instead.
interactions: On initial load, read bookingId from the URL query string; if missing, show an error state and do not call booking-related interfaces.
On initial load, call getAuthStatus; if isAuthenticated is false, show an authentication-required state and do not call getBookingDetail or getRescheduleOptions.
On successful auth and valid bookingId, call getBookingDetail(bookingId) to populate all booking detail components.
When the user clicks the breadcrumb or a dedicated back link, navigate to my_bookings.html without additional query parameters.
When the user clicks the reschedule action for an eligible booking, update the URL to indicate reschedule mode, call getRescheduleOptions(bookingId), and expand the reschedule UI.

When getRescheduleOptions returns, render earliestAllowedNewDate and suggestedSessions; disable or visually mark any sessions that do not meet policy conditions (if applicable based on the returned data).
When the user selects a suggested session, store the selected classSessionId in page state and optionally sync it to the URL as a query parameter to preserve the selection across reloads.
